title: Data Breach at Indian Supplier Tata Electronics Exposes iPhone 18 Pro Details and Photos
blurb: A ransomware group breached Apple supplier Tata Electronics and posted stolen files to the dark web, including component lists and photos of the unreleased iPhone 18 Pro. The leaked documents map hundreds of parts to specific suppliers, data Apple keeps off its public supplier list, exposing its sourcing leverage and single-supplier vulnerabilities to rivals and counterfeiters.

title: Quality Assurance Agent: Reimagining Software Quality with AI-Driven Autonomous Testing
blurb: LinkedIn built a Quality Assurance Agent that uses vision-language models to navigate its iOS, Android, and web apps, perceive the UI like a human, and run end-to-end tests without relying on brittle selectors or code identifiers. The system has already flagged over 200 valid bugs and caught regressions in revenue-impacting flows, while letting non-engineers author tests in natural language.

title: Top 1 Million Analysis – June 2026: Ten Years of Web Security
blurb: Scott Helme's decade-anniversary crawl of the Tranco Top 1 Million finds HTTPS now near-universal but security-header adoption still stalled.

title: Samsung Electronics, SK Hynix shares tumble over 9% as chip rout spreads from Wall Street
blurb: Samsung Electronics and SK Hynix closed down 9.06% and 14.57% Thursday, dragging South Korea's Kospi lower as a chip selloff spread from Wall Street's overnight Nasdaq slump. The rout hit chipmakers across Asia, with China's SMIC falling more than 11% and Micron down over 10% stateside, even as SK Hynix announced a 100 trillion won ($64.37B) domestic investment plan.

title: WhatsApp’s Username Revamp Faces India Test Over Fraud Risks
blurb: India asked Meta to delay WhatsApp's new user-chosen username feature over concerns it could enable online fraud.

title: Amazon’s new Fire Sticks are turning sideloading into a thing of the past
blurb: Amazon's new Vega OS Fire TV Sticks block the consumer sideloading and custom launchers that let owners install outside apps for years, starting with the Fire TV Stick 4K Select and all future sticks. Amazon cites malware and piracy risks, but the shift also cuts supported apps from roughly 40,000 on Android-based Fire OS to about 3,000 while tightening its grip on the ad-heavy home screen.

title: Vantor offers up-to-date imagery with WorldView 3D
blurb: Vantor, the company formerly known as Maxar Intelligence, released WorldView 3D, a satellite-based service that delivers updated high-definition 3D maps at up to 15-centimeter resolution. The Rapid tier tasks satellites and returns 50-centimeter 3D maps within 24 hours, targeting intelligence analysts, disaster response, and GPS-denied drone navigation without aircraft or drone flyovers.

title: Pine64 launch $50 smart speaker for Home Assistant tinkerers
blurb: Pine64 launched PineVoice, a $50 open-hardware smart speaker built on a Bouffalo Lab BL606P RISC-V chip with Wi-Fi, Bluetooth 5.0, and Zigbee, dual microphones, and local wake-word detection. It runs the Wyoming Satellite protocol to act as a local mic and speaker for self-hosted Home Assistant setups, targeting tinkerers who want an Echo Dot alternative without a cloud ecosystem.

title: Musk Says Grok 4.5 Entered Private Beta
blurb: Musk says Grok 4.5, built on the 1.5T V9 model, is in private beta at SpaceX and Tesla with near-Opus evals.

title: Google Cloud will sell specialist AI models built for science
blurb: Google Cloud will begin selling SandboxAQ's specialist "large quantitative models" through its marketplace, offering AI trained on scientific equations and lab data rather than text for drug discovery, materials science, and semiconductor work. Researchers can pair the models with Gemini for reasoning and interface, and Google bundled the move with Gemini for Science, positioning its cloud as the default home for high-value enterprise and research R&D.

title: Amazon launches new $1 billion FDE org, following OpenAI and Anthropic
blurb: Amazon Web Services launched an internal organization for AI-focused forward-deployed engineers, committing $1B in internal resources to embed staff inside customer companies and deploy purpose-built agents. The move follows FDE joint ventures from OpenAI and Anthropic, valued at $4B and $1.5B, and signals that the AI bottleneck has shifted from model quality to getting deployments live inside enterprises.

title: A field experiment of social influence and behavioral contagion with bots on Reddit
blurb: A Reddit field experiment tested whether symbolic awards from apparent humans and bots could nudge users toward more activity, finding that awards failed to boost engagement and that bot-issued lottery awards actually reduced it. The authors argue users are largely resilient to crude algorithmic manipulation but likely not to schemes that convincingly mimic human conversation, making transparent labeling of automated agents essential.

title: No discs, more problems: What Sony’s all-digital PlayStation means for gamers and the industry
blurb: Sony announced that as of January 2028, all future PlayStation games will be digital-only, ending physical discs, trade-ins, and lending across its platforms. The move arrives faster than analysts predicted and erases roughly $1.9B in annual physical sales, cutting per-unit costs ahead of an all-digital PS6 while turning owned game libraries into revocable licenses.

title: Quantum Systems More Than Doubles Valuation to $8 Billion
blurb: Quantum Systems raised $1.2 billion in a new funding round, more than doubling the German drone startup's valuation to roughly $8 billion post-money. The deal underscores investors' growing appetite for defense-related bets, as European militaries expand spending and startups building unmanned aerial systems draw fresh capital at rapidly climbing valuations.

title: SK Hynix will spend $51bn on a new NAND factory to catch the AI memory wave
blurb: SK Hynix will spend 80 trillion won ($51.5B) on M17, a new NAND flash factory in Cheongju, South Korea, with production targeted for early 2029. The investment, which rises to about $64B once an advanced packaging plant is included, bets that flash storage still needs major new capacity as AI datacenter buildouts tighten memory supply into 2027.

title: Understanding dynamic resource allocation in Kubernetes
blurb: Dynamic Resource Allocation (DRA) reached GA in Kubernetes v1.35, giving clusters a standard API for allocating specialized hardware like GPUs to workloads. This post walks through configuring DRA on a multi-node cluster with NVIDIA's dra-driver-nvidia-gpu, which recently moved into Kubernetes SIGs and dropped its Beta label.

title: Is One Layer Enough? Training A Single Transformer Layer Can Match Full-Parameter RL Training
blurb: This paper challenges the assumption that reinforcement learning post-training benefits all transformer layers equally, showing that training a single layer can recover most of the gains from full-parameter RL and sometimes exceed them. Across seven Qwen3 and Qwen2.5 models, three RL algorithms, and tasks spanning math, code, and agentic decisions, the authors find RL gains concentrate in a few middle-stack layers, with rankings that stay stable across datasets and methods.

title: Early look at Anthropic's Claude Science app for researchers
blurb: Anthropic released Claude Science, a research workbench for life-sciences and related fields that runs on local machines, remote clusters, or over SSH and is in beta for Pro, Max, Team, and Enterprise users. It bundles over 60 specialized tools across genomics, proteomics, cheminformatics, and structural biology, with reviewer agents that flag citation errors and calculation mistakes and attach code and environment details to every artifact for reproducibility.

title: AI Agent Exploits Langflow RCE to Automate Database Ransomware Attack
blurb: Sysdig's Threat Research Team reported what it believes is the first ransomware attack run end-to-end by an AI agent, tracking the operator as JADEPUFFER. The agent broke in through CVE-2025-3248, a patched Langflow authentication flaw, then harvested credentials, pivoted to a MySQL and Nacos server, and encrypted 1,342 Nacos configurations before leaving a Bitcoin ransom note.

title: Popping the GPU Bubble
blurb: Photon hides GPU bubbles using pipelined decoding, which overlaps GPU and CPU work instead of running them as a sequential baton pass. In standard autoregressive decoding, the GPU finishes one token's math, then sits idle while the CPU handles fixed-cost housekeeping like selecting requests, setting up metadata, and committing the chosen token. Photon launches the next forward pass while the current token is still being committed, keeping the GPU busy across the loop.

title: GSRQ: Gain-Shape Residual Quantization for Sub-1-bit KV Cache
blurb: GSRQ is a KV cache quantization method that pushes storage into the sub-1-bit regime by replacing standard ℓ2 K-means with Gain-Shape K-means, which preserves directional fidelity by avoiding the centroid shrinkage that weakens angular alignment. On LLaMA-3-8B at 1-bit, GSRQ raises average LongBench accuracy from 11.34 to 33.54, a 22.20-point gain over the VQLLM baseline.

title: Message Passing Enables Efficient Reasoning
blurb: This paper introduces Message Passing Language Models (MPLMs), a framework where parallel LLM reasoning threads communicate directly through lightweight send and receive primitives rather than the transient fork-join pattern. MPLMs cut communication costs by avoiding redundant context sharing and allow threads to terminate early on partial peer information, requiring asymptotically smaller context than serial CoT and parallel fork-join on Sudoku, 3-SAT, and long-context question answering.

title: U.S. lifting export control restrictions on Anthropic’s Mythos, Fable
blurb: Anthropic said its Fable 5 and Mythos 5 models are publicly available again after reaching an agreement with the Commerce Department to deploy them with new guardrails and jailbreak classifiers. The export controls, which had blocked foreign and U.S. access, followed an Amazon report claiming to have jailbroken Fable's cybersecurity capabilities, though Anthropic noted equivalent models like ChatGPT 5.5 and Claude Opus 4.8 could identify the same vulnerabilities.

title: HARC: Coupling Harmfulness and Refusal Directions for Robust Safety Alignment
blurb: HARC is a fine-tuning method for LLM safety that couples the internal harmfulness and refusal directions across both prompt and response token positions, targeting how jailbreaks suppress one direction before generation begins. Because the intervention stays confined to the harmfulness-refusal subspace, it preserves general capability and avoids over-refusal, beating six training-time and inference-time baselines and transferring across five model families and two scales without architecture-specific tuning.

title: QuasiMoTTo: Quasi-Monte Carlo Test-Time Scaling
blurb: QuasiMoTTo is a test-time scaling method that replaces independent parallel samples with correlated but marginally exact ones, using quasi-Monte Carlo to spread autoregressive samples more evenly across the output space. Across four reasoning benchmarks it matches i.i.d. pass@k accuracy with 25-47% fewer samples and matches GRPO reinforcement learning performance with 50% fewer training steps.

title: Anthropic is removing its covert code for catching Chinese competitors
blurb: Anthropic said it will disable a covert steganography system it had embedded in its model outputs to detect when Chinese rivals were distilling or copying its models. The hidden watermarking scheme flagged competitors reusing Anthropic-generated text, but the company is now removing it after the mechanism came to light.

title: OpenAI floats giving Trump administration 5 percent cut of AI boom
blurb: OpenAI has floated giving the US government a 5% ownership stake to ease tensions with the Trump administration and blunt public backlash against AI, according to the Financial Times. Based on OpenAI's $852B valuation, the stake would be worth roughly $42.6B, and the proposal reportedly would require other US AI companies to grant the government similar stakes.

title: Safari’s new MCP server lets coding agents inspect and debug websites
blurb: Apple released the Safari MCP server in Safari Technology Preview 247, connecting coding agents directly to a Safari browser window to inspect how code renders. Agents can read page content, console logs, and network requests, capture screenshots, and interact with page elements, replacing the manual browser-prompt-agent debugging loop.

title: Text AI watermarks will always be trivial to remove
blurb: The author argues that text AI watermarks will always be trivial to remove, even as the EU AI Act's Article 50 begins requiring AI outputs to be detectable as artificially generated in August 2026. Unlike images, text is a compressed medium where any watermark-inducing change degrades quality or wastes reasoning time. The post surveys the partial solutions labs are betting on, including Google's SynthID and quiet Unicode tricks from OpenAI and Anthropic.

title: Anthropic restores Claude Fable 5 as US lifts export controls — single filter now blocks prompt that could identify software vulnerabilities and write code to exploit them
blurb: Anthropic restored global access to Claude Fable 5 a day after the U.S. Department of Commerce withdrew the export controls it imposed on June 12th, ending an 18-day standoff. The fix was a single classifier that blocks one Amazon-flagged prompting technique in over 99% of cases and reroutes flagged requests to Opus 4.8, after testing confirmed models like GPT-5.5 and Kimi K2.7 could identify the same vulnerabilities.

title: The State-Prediction Separation Hypothesis
blurb: This paper introduces the state-prediction separation hypothesis, arguing that Transformers hurt performance by using one forward computation stream to both predict the next token and store state for future predictions. A variant that splits these roles into two streams improved data and compute efficiency across pretraining scales, lowering validation loss and beating standard Transformers by 2-3 percentage points on average on downstream tasks.

title: How we keep GPUs reliable across Databricks AI
blurb: Databricks published the first post in a series on keeping GPUs reliable across its AI training workloads, covering the failure modes, workloads, and multi-stage health check system it uses at scale. It groups most GPU failures into three categories, crashed jobs, silent slowdowns, and numerical corruption, and argues the harder cases are the silent ones, where jobs finish with wrong numbers or run degraded for hours without surfacing an obvious error like an NCCL watchdog timeout.

title: Senior SWE-Bench: open-source benchmark that assesses agents as senior engineers
blurb: Senior SWE-Bench is an open-source benchmark that evaluates coding agents on under-specified feature and bug tasks, using a validation agent that writes behavioral tests and quality metrics based on observed codebase practices. Claude Opus 4.8 tops the leaderboard at 24%, with every frontier model failing to solve over 75% of tasks at senior-level correctness and taste.

title: Beyond Document Grounding: Span-Level Hallucination Detection over Code, Tool Output, and Documents
blurb: This paper introduces a unified benchmark for span-level hallucination detection over source code, developer-tool output, and structured documents, extending RAG evaluation beyond natural-language evidence. The fine-tuned Qwen3.5-2B detector reaches 0.689 span-F1 on the unified set and 0.60 on code-agent output, well ahead of LettuceDetect-large at 0.17 and the strongest zero-shot LLM judges at 0.22.

title: RACORN-1: Adaptive Recall-Preserving Speedup for Low-Selectivity Filtered Vector Search
blurb: RACORN-1 is an in-place extension of the ACORN-1 filtered vector search algorithm that fixes recall collapse below 1% selectivity by repurposing filter-failing nodes as transient bridges to detour around severed graph paths. Across four datasets up to 40M vectors, it delivers 9-26x latency reductions over HNSW in the 1%-0.3% range while recovering recall from as low as 0.03 to 0.77-0.98.

title: Are Performance-Optimization Benchmarks Reliably Measuring Coding Agents?
blurb: This paper audits three repository-level performance-optimization benchmarks (GSO, SWE-Perf, and SWE-fficiency) and finds their leaderboard scores unreliable. Replaying official reference patches across four Google Cloud machine types, the authors found the patches satisfied original validity rules in only 39/102 GSO, 11/140 SWE-Perf, and 411/498 SWE-fficiency tasks, with SWE-Perf especially fragile due to near-zero runtime changes. Rankings also flip with the scoring rule, disagreeing on 9 of 28 pairwise comparisons between shared submissions.

title: AutoMem: Automated Learning of Memory as a Cognitive Skill
blurb: AutoMem is a framework that treats LLM memory management as a trainable skill, using one loop where a strong model revises the agent's memory structure and another that turns the agent's own good memory decisions into training signal. Across three long-horizon games (Crafter, MiniHack, and NetHack), optimizing memory alone improved a 32B open-weight agent's performance 2x to 4x, making it competitive with Claude Opus 4.5 and Gemini 3.1 Pro Thinking.

title: Beyond the Prompt: Jailbreaking Function-Calling LLMs via Simulated Moderation Traces
blurb: This paper presents SMT, a black-box jailbreak framework that exploits function-calling LLMs by simulating a legitimate moderation-auditing workflow across multiple turns rather than attacking at the prompt level. By fabricating a moderation frame that treats safety refusals as execution failures, SMT gradually weakens safety constraints and achieved the highest attack success rate against commercial models from five providers, showing that prompt-level sanitization alone cannot defend tool-enabled systems.

title: The Model Organism Lottery: Model Organism Interpretability Strongly Depends on Training Methodology
blurb: This paper builds 54 model organisms from OLMo2-1B and gemma-3-1b-it using seven training techniques, then benchmarks activation oracles, activation steering, logit lens, and sparse autoencoders against each variant. The authors find that interpretability depends heavily on training objective, target behavior, and architecture, and that more realistic integrated training often yields less interpretable models, casting doubt on current model organisms as interpretability proxies.

title: Cloudflare sets a September 15 deadline for AI companies to differentiate their web crawlers into search, AI training, and AI agents or face being blocked (Samantha Elkins/NBC News)
blurb: Cloudflare set a September 15 deadline for AI companies to sort their web crawlers into three categories: search, AI training, and AI agents. Crawlers that fail to declare their purpose by the deadline risk being blocked, giving publishers finer control over which bots can access their content and for what use.

title: DiScoFormer: One transformer for density and score, across distributions
blurb: Allen AI introduced DiScoFormer, a transformer that estimates both the density and the score of a distribution from a set of data points in a single forward pass without retraining. The model uses cross-attention to evaluate density and score at any point, with a shared backbone and two output heads that exploit the relationship between score and the gradient of log-density to stay accurate as dimensionality grows.

title: Meta pops 8% as company makes cloud push to sell excess AI compute power capacity
blurb: Meta is building a cloud business to sell its excess AI computing capacity to outside customers, sending shares up 8% on Wednesday. The move could recoup part of the up to $145B in capex Meta plans to spend this year, while pushing it into a market dominated by Amazon, Microsoft, Google, and CoreWeave.

title: FurnitureVLA: Learning Long-Horizon Bimanual Furniture Assembly with Vision-Language-Action Model
blurb: FurnitureVLA is a vision-language-action framework for real-scale bimanual furniture assembly, spanning tasks with up to 7 subtasks and 1,550 control steps. Its progress-enhanced model jointly predicts actions and a continuous progress signal for automatic subtask transitions, raising average simulation success from 48% to 80% across three furniture types with only a 16% drop on hardware.

title: OpenAI has reportedly offered Washington a 5% stake worth $42.6bn
blurb: OpenAI has reportedly offered the US government a 5% equity stake worth roughly $42.6B at its $852B valuation, according to the Financial Times. The shares would be donated rather than sold through a proposed "public wealth fund," a vehicle that could also draw stakes from Anthropic, Google, and Meta to distribute AI's economic gains to citizens.

title: ZO-Act: Efficient Zeroth-Order Fine-Tuning via One-Shot Activation-Informed Low-Rank Subspaces
blurb: ZO-Act is a zeroth-order fine-tuning method that restricts weight perturbations to a fixed low-rank subspace derived from input activations, computed once per linear layer at initialization. By optimizing only lightweight coefficient matrices with forward-only loss evaluations, it lowers estimator variance, works with Adam-style optimizers, and supports quantized models, showing consistent gains over strong baselines on Llama-3-8B, OPT-13B, and INT4 Llama-3-8B.

title: Right in the Right Way: LM Training with Verifiable Rewards and Human Demonstrations
blurb: This paper proposes an adversarial generator-discriminator framework that augments RL with verifiable rewards (RLVR) using a learned signal from human demonstrations, targeting the style, structure, and diversity that objective scoring alone neglects. Across bug fixing, story generation, and a reward-hacking benchmark, the method preserves RLVR's accuracy while cutting edit distance, raising win rate, and nearly eliminating reward hacking.

title: Kimi K2.7 Code is generally available in GitHub Copilot
blurb: GitHub Copilot made Kimi K2.7 Code generally available, the first open-weight model selectable in the Copilot model picker. The model is hosted by GitHub on Microsoft Azure and billed at provider list pricing, rolling out first to Pro, Pro+, and Max plans before reaching Business and Enterprise in the coming weeks.

title: $\text{Log}_\text{b}$Quant: Quantizing Language Models in Logarithmic Space
blurb: LogbQuant is a logarithmic weight-quantization method with adjustable bases that adapts to the high-magnitude, low-frequency weights that trip up uniform codebooks. At 4-bit precision, it outperforms asymmetric linear quantization at tensor-wise granularity across several benchmarks while delivering moderate speedup and high memory savings, making language models viable on consumer-grade GPUs.

title: Muon as a Residual Connection
blurb: This paper proposes a mechanistic interpretation of Muon, arguing the optimizer can be understood as an implicit residual connection during training. The authors show that orthogonalizing the update trades some immediate gradient fidelity for better representation preservation, letting a layer fit its local target more slowly while producing features that downstream layers exploit more easily.

title: Staleness-Learning Rate Scaling Laws for Asynchronous RLHF
blurb: This paper analyzes how stale rollouts affect asynchronous GRPO for RLHF, making the behavior policy explicit in the surrogate objective and proving that staleness adds a per-step gradient bias of order O(S·η), where S is the maximum rollout lag and η the learning rate. The authors derive a two-constraint stability condition on the learning rate, explaining why the maximum stable rate appears only weakly dependent on staleness in horizon-limited training regimes.

title: Clinician-Level Agreement Without Clinical Caution: LLM Evaluator Limits in Medical AI Benchmarking
blurb: This paper introduces MedQADE, the first standardized open-response clinical benchmark for German, with 3,800 items scored by ten physicians and nine LLM judges. The top evaluator, Gemini 3 Flash, matched the physician agreement ceiling (kappa 0.694 vs 0.709) but showed near-absent clinical caution, assigning definitive scores on every item while physicians abstained more as difficulty rose. The authors also found lineage bias, where models scored architectural siblings more favorably, arguing statistical alignment does not guarantee evaluator reliability.

title: Logit-Contribution Scoring Identifies Non-Literal Retrieval Heads
blurb: This paper introduces Logit-Contribution Scoring (LOCOS), a write-aware detector that identifies attention heads performing non-literal retrieval by projecting each head's output-value circuit onto the answer-token unembedding direction. Ablating the top 50 LOCOS heads on Qwen3-8B collapses NoLiMa ROUGE-L from 0.401 to 0.000, where the strongest attention-based baseline retains 0.292, while parametric recall and arithmetic reasoning stay at baseline.

title: KidnapRAG: A Black-Box Attack for Hijacking Reasoning in Agentic Retrieval-Augmented Generation Systems
blurb: KidnapRAG is a black-box poisoning attack that hijacks the multi-step reasoning chain in Agentic RAG systems using only externally retrievable documents, without access to prompts, retrievers, or model parameters. The attack plants three role-specific documents, Bait, Chain-Link, and Mal-Ins, to attract initial retrieval, force query reformulation, and supply attacker-controlled evidence, outperforming existing poisoning baselines across multiple frameworks and LLM backbones.

title: SWE-Doctor: Guiding Software Engineering Agents with Runtime Diagnosis from Multi-Faceted Bug Reproduction Tests
blurb: SWE-Doctor is a software issue resolution agent that guides patch generation using runtime diagnoses built from multi-faceted bug reproduction tests, each targeting a different behavioral requirement in the issue. Across five LLM backends, it reaches average resolution rates of 75.7% on SWE-bench Verified and 59.4% on SWE-bench Pro, improving the harder Pro benchmark by 8.0 to 8.9 points over baseline agents.

title: Distill to Detect: Exposing Stealth Biases in LLMs through Cartridge Distillation
blurb: This paper introduces Distill to Detect (D2D), a method that surfaces hidden preferential biases in LLMs by distilling the distributional shift between a suspected model and its base into a cartridge, a KV-cache prefix adapter that concentrates the divergence and amplifies the bias into generated text. The authors show D2D reliably detects stealth biases across multiple types and explain its efficacy through a Fisher-weighted projection of the logit distribution shift.

title: OpenAI Proposes Giving 5% Stake to US Government: FT
blurb: OpenAI has begun preliminary talks about giving the US government a 5% stake in the company, according to a Financial Times report. Executives proposed the move as part of a broader arrangement under which Washington would hold 5% of each of the leading American AI developers.

title: (A)I Sees What You Don't: Exploiting New Attack Surfaces in Third-Party Mobile Agents
blurb: This paper identifies two attack surfaces unique to third-party mobile agents powered by vision-language models: a Screen Perception surface that exploits the gap between human and machine vision, and a Misused Channel surface that intercepts the agent's execution pipeline. Across five popular agent frameworks, the authors implement seven attacks, including subliminal text injection and host PC command injection, showing a malicious app can achieve arbitrary command execution without any permissions while staying visually invisible to users.

title: RepoRescue: An Empirical Study of LLM Agents on Whole-Repository Compatibility Rescue
blurb: RepoRescue is a benchmark of 193 Python and 122 Java repositories that tests whether LLM agents can restore abandoned codebases after their runtimes and dependencies drift out of compatibility. Under runtime enforcement that blocks test-file edits, Kimi rescued 41.5% of repositories, the union of systems reached 62.7%, and GPT-5.2 through Codex solved all 14 tasks requiring coordinated cross-file changes while every Claude Code system passed at most two.

title: Meta shares jump 9% on reported plan to offer AI infrastructure services
blurb: Meta reportedly plans to rent out excess AI infrastructure not consumed by its internal workloads, sending its shares up 8.8% while CoreWeave fell 13.9% and Nebius dropped 17%. The move would open capacity from a capex budget of up to $145B this year, and Meta is weighing whether to sell raw compute, hosted models, or access to its Muse Spark LLM.

title: How we used DSPy to turn AI evaluations into better responses in Dash chat
blurb: Dropbox used DSPy to turn its LLM-as-judge evaluations into a feedback loop that improves its Dash chat agent. The team first calibrated the judges against human-labeled examples using optimization algorithms like GEPA and MIPROv2, then used those judges to optimize the agent's system prompt, cutting incomplete answers and token usage without degrading answer quality.

title: Language-Critique Imitation Learning from Suboptimal Demonstrations
blurb: This paper introduces a language-critique framework for imitation learning from suboptimal demonstrations, replacing scalar signals like confidence estimates and discriminator scores with natural language labels that describe task progress, flag failure modes, and give corrective guidance. The authors train policies directly on these structured signals via a language-critique loss, yielding LC-BC and LC-DP variants that outperform imitation learning and offline RL baselines across navigation, manipulation, and gameplay tasks.

title: Gemini Spark, Google’s agentic assistant, is now available on Mac
blurb: Google released Gemini Spark for macOS in beta, adding file access, real-time topic tracking, custom MCP support, and integrations with Google Tasks, Keep, Canva, Dropbox, and OpenTable. The desktop launch positions Spark against Claude Desktop and Microsoft Copilot, though it remains limited to Google AI Ultra subscribers in the US for now.

title: The Orbital Data Center Hype Machine Is Already in Orbit
blurb: An IEEE Spectrum cover story argues that orbital data centers are nowhere close to viability, despite Elon Musk's claim that space will be the lowest-cost place for AI compute within three years. SpaceX has filed with the FCC for a constellation of up to 1 million satellites, but deploying that many on Starship would take roughly 16,666 dedicated launches, and matching Starlink's build rate would need about 25 years. Starcloud, a competing startup planning 88,000 satellites, has so far put a single Nvidia H100 in orbit.

title: OpenAI, Anthropic backer MGX raises one of the biggest AI funds ever as it closes at $49 billion
blurb: Abu Dhabi's MGX closed a $49B fund to back AI companies, one of the largest sector-focused vehicles ever and above its initial $45B target. The fund has already backed 14 companies, including co-leading Anthropic's $30B round and OpenAI's $122B raise, and plans to invest across the stack from semiconductors to AI infrastructure.

title: CausalMix: Data Mixture as Causal Inference for Language Model Training
blurb: CausalMix reframes LLM data mixture optimization as a causal inference problem, treating data-pool statistics as covariates and domain mixture as the treatment to estimate Conditional Average Treatment Effects. After fitting on 512 runs of Qwen2.5-0.5B, it extrapolates an optimal mixture for an 800K-example pool to train a 7B model, outperforming RegMix without costly retraining when the data pool shifts.

title: Theoria: Rewrite-Acceptability Verification over Informal Reasoning States
blurb: Theoria is a verification architecture that rewrites a candidate solution into a sequence of typed, independently auditable state transitions, each licensed by an explicit justification such as a citation, computation, or given fact. On HLE-Verified Gold it certifies 105 of 185 expert problems at 91.4% strict precision, and its "completeness of change" invariant surfaces hidden premises and fabricated citations that opaque LLM judges miss, catching 94.7% of poisoned proofs versus 83.2%.

title: Nvidia Offers Revenue Sharing Model for Aspiring AI Startups
blurb: Nvidia introduced a revenue-sharing model that grants developers token credits for its AI hardware in exchange for a share of their future sales. The program is aimed at widening access to Nvidia's compute for early-stage startups that cannot afford large upfront GPU costs, tying the company's returns to its customers' growth.

title: The MCP Release Candidate Survival Guide: Apps, Auth, Deprecations, and Tool Schemas
blurb: The MCP 2026-07-28 release candidate, locked on May 21 and finalized July 28, is the largest specification revision since the protocol launched and makes MCP stateless at the protocol layer. This guide walks through the migration work, covering transport assumptions that hide state in the connection, server-to-client request flows that now retry with input responses, and MCP Apps as interactive surfaces.

title: Query-Centric Optimization of AI Workflows via Approximate Query Processing and Proxy Models
blurb: This paper reframes expensive AI workflows, from LLM post-training to agentic reasoning, as declarative queries and applies two classical database techniques to cut costly model calls without touching the underlying models. Approximate query processing samples records and stops early once a confidence interval stabilizes, while a lightweight CPU-resident decision tree pre-filters easy cases, together reducing oracle calls by 60-90% and cutting reward-model scoring time up to 19x on structured tasks.

title: What the AI patch gap means for enterprise security
blurb: Anthropic's Claude Mythos Preview scanned more than 23,000 open-source code paths over roughly nine weeks and routed 1,596 verified vulnerabilities to maintainers, with outside firms confirming a 90.8% true-positive rate. Discovery ran at about 25 verified bugs a day while credited fixes landed at closer to 1.5, a 16.5-to-1 gap that Tuskira calls the vulnerability deficit.

title: ChatGPT Thinking mode changes which brands get cited
blurb: ChatGPT's high-reasoning Thinking mode behaves like a separate search surface for brand visibility, sharing only 25.6% of cited domains with minimal reasoning across 100 prompts, according to a Semrush analysis with Kevin Indig. High reasoning ran nearly five times as many web searches, lifted citation rates from 50% to 68%, and cut Reddit's share from 15% to 7% while government and academic sources rose from 1.9% to 8.8%.

title: World from Motion: Generative Dynamic Gaussian Reconstruction from Monocular Video
blurb: World from Motion is a method that generates freely renderable dynamic 3D Gaussian representations from monocular video, conditioning a video model on dense, pixel-aligned renderings of appearance, geometry, and 3D scene motion to correct artifacts and fill in missing regions. At test time it distills those generations back into a single consistent dynamic 3DGS, setting a new state of the art in 4D reconstruction and generalizing to in-the-wild videos with large viewpoint changes.

title: MemSyco-Bench: Benchmarking Sycophancy in Agent Memory
blurb: MemSyco-Bench is a benchmark for measuring memory-induced sycophancy in LLM agents, the tendency to over-align with a user's stored preferences at the expense of factual accuracy. It spans five tasks that test whether agents can reject memory as factual evidence, respect its applicable scope, resolve conflicts between memory and objective evidence, track memory updates, and apply valid memory for personalization.

title: Adversarial Pragmatics for AI Safety Evaluation: A Benchmark for Instruction Conflict, Embedded Commands, and Policy Ambiguity
blurb: This paper introduces adversarial pragmatics, a benchmark and annotation protocol for evaluating language model behavior under instruction conflict, embedded commands, quotation, scope ambiguity, and indirect speech acts. It provides a linguistically controlled taxonomy, an 18-item seed benchmark with validator-enforced metadata, and an expert protocol separating task success, policy compliance, safety risk, and evaluator confidence to validate safety evals and LLM judges.

title: Diffusion-GR2: Diffusion Generative Reasoning Re-ranker
blurb: Diffusion-GR2 converts an autoregressive generative reasoning re-ranker into a block-diffusion model that decodes many positions in parallel, cutting the slow token-by-token inference of chain-of-thought re-ranking. The recipe combines conversion fine-tuning, on-policy distillation, and a reinforcement-learning stage to recover near-parity with the AR model on Amazon Beauty while raising decode throughput by 2.4x to 3.5x.

title: Skills Are Not Islands: Measuring Dependency and Risk in Agent Skill Supply Chains
blurb: This paper introduces Agent Skill Supply Chains (ASSCs) and SkillDepAnalyzer, a tool that models LLM agent skills as dependency-bearing artifacts and reconstructs their skill-package-service dependency graphs from natural-language evidence. Applied to over 1.43 million skills, it outperformed LLM and SBOM baselines, surfaced malicious skills persisting through transitive dependencies, and motivated recommendations for typed dependency manifests and lockfile-like records.

title: ZCode – Harness for GLM-5.2
blurb: ZCode is an agentic coding harness optimized for GLM-5.2 that lets developers plan, code, review, and deploy across reasoning, coding, and multi-agent collaboration workflows. It supports 20+ coding tools, uses Goals for continuous planning and verification, and can be started or steered remotely from WeChat, Feishu, or Telegram.

title: Quantum vs. Classical Machine Learning: A Unified Empirical Comparison
blurb: This paper benchmarks quantum machine learning against classical baselines across seven model pairs spanning supervised learning and reinforcement learning. The quantum models do not surpass their classical counterparts on prediction performance, policy stability, or training time. The authors note QML still shows promise for filtering noise and controlling false positives, and outline the hardware, convergence, and training-efficiency hurdles that remain.

title: Can Agents Generalize to the Open World? Unveiling the Fragility of Static Training in Tool Use
blurb: This paper formalizes OpenAgent, a setting that tests LLM tool-use agents under distributional shifts across query, action, observation, and domain dimensions in a controlled sandbox. The authors show that agents trained with both supervised fine-tuning and reinforcement learning degrade under these shifts, and propose Perturbation-Augmented Fine-Tuning, a disturbance-based SFT intervention that improves robustness in realistic environments.

title: Neural Certificate Pricing for Combinatorial Optimization Problems
blurb: This paper introduces Neural Certificate Pricing (NCP), an unsupervised method for combinatorial optimization that trains a network to predict certificate-level dual prices while a structured recovery layer builds the induced primal solution. Across three problem classes, NCP either beats state-of-the-art neural baselines by large margins or matches them at a fraction of the compute, with stronger out-of-distribution generalization.

title: Knowledge-Enhanced Agentic Vulnerability Repair
blurb: KeaRepair is an agentic automated vulnerability repair system that grounds patch generation in verified program facts and knowledge retrieved from historical vulnerability-patch pairs, using ReAct-style diagnosis and a closed-loop validation cycle of compilation, PoC replay, and test execution. Paired with Gemini-3.1-Pro, it repaired 46 of 55 reproducible C/C++ vulnerabilities for an 83.64% repair rate and fixed six that no baseline could address.

title: ChatGPT developer OpenAI said to discuss offering U.S. government a 5% stake: FT
blurb: OpenAI discussed granting the U.S. government a 5% equity stake as part of a proposal to share AI-generated wealth with the public, the Financial Times reported. The plan, raised by Sam Altman in early talks with Trump administration officials, draws on Alaska's Permanent Fund model, remains conceptual, and would likely require Congressional approval.

title: Antaeus: Hunting Repository-Level Logic Vulnerabilities via Context-Grounded LLM Reasoning
blurb: Antaeus is a framework that detects repository-level logic vulnerabilities by grounding LLM reasoning in whole-repo context, prioritizing functions with lightweight security signals before running costly analysis on the code that matters. On 28 repositories with confirmed logic bugs, it detected and explained 15 vulnerabilities, outperforming function-level and agentic baselines at comparable token usage and cost.

title: When Classic Cache Policies Fail: Learning-Augmented Replacement for Semantic Retrieval Buffers
blurb: This paper formalizes semantic cache replacement for LLM retrieval buffers as an online problem with switching costs, showing that classic heuristics like LRU and LFU consistently underperform a naive FIFO baseline because semantic workloads lack temporal locality and frequency concentration. The authors propose SOLAR, a learning-augmented policy that proves a constant competitive ratio of at most 3 and delivers 5-75% relative improvement over FIFO at tight cache sizes.

title: SoftBank Plans to Rent AI Compute in US at 10-Gigawatt Scale
blurb: SoftBank and its telecom unit will begin renting AI compute to US companies next fiscal year, tapping a growing pipeline of data centers to reach 10-gigawatt scale. The move puts SoftBank in direct competition with GPU-cloud providers like CoreWeave and Nebius as demand for rentable AI capacity climbs.

title: AGC-Bench: Measuring Artificial General Creativity
blurb: AGC-Bench is an artificial general creativity benchmark spanning 78 datasets across brainstorming, problem solving, STEM, narrative, figurative language, and humor, paired with AGC-Judge, an open-weight Qwen3-30B scorer calibrated to correct LLM-judge bias. Factor analysis across 83 LLMs recovers a single creativity factor that explains 81.5% of variance and is separable from reasoning, though the top human still outscores the top model.

title: ReShift: Aha-Moment-Driven Reasoning-Level Backdoor Attacks on Vision-Language Models
blurb: ReShift is a backdoor attack framework for vision-language models that hijacks the internal chain-of-thought trajectory rather than just the final output, keeping the poisoned reasoning traces coherent and hard to detect. It combines a poisoned reasoning-aware data pipeline with joint supervised and reinforcement optimization, achieving high attack success rates while preserving clean-task performance and evading existing defenses.

title: Group-invariant Coresets for Data-efficient Active Learning
blurb: GRINCO is a group-invariant coreset framework for active learning that performs sample acquisition in the quotient space induced by a transformation group, selecting on orbits rather than raw samples to avoid wasting labeling budget on transformed duplicates. It combines quotient-space k-center selection with orbit-averaged invariant training, and on image benchmarks with rotation-induced redundancy it achieves stronger label efficiency than conventional coreset baselines.

title: DART-VLN: Test-Time Memory Decay and Anti-Loop Regularization for Discrete Vision-Language Navigation
blurb: DART-VLN is a training-free, test-time control framework for discrete vision-language navigation that pairs Test-Time Memory Decay, which down-weights stale evidence at memory readout, with Anti-Loop Regularization, a next-hop penalty that discourages immediate reversals. On R2R and REVERIE the combined method produces shorter trajectories, lower runtime, and better navigation quality while adding no new parameters and leaving the frozen backbone unchanged.

title: Nvidia promises to financially backstop young cloud providers like Firmus that rent out its AI chips, in exchange for a revenue share through a new program (Phoebe Liu/The Information)
blurb: Nvidia launched a program to financially backstop young cloud providers like Firmus that rent out its AI chips, in exchange for a share of their revenue. The arrangement uses Nvidia's balance sheet to help smaller companies afford its expensive GPUs, expanding the pool of buyers while giving Nvidia a path to recoup some of that spending.

title: 14x faster embeddings: how we rebuilt the ONNX path in Manticore
blurb: Manticore Search 27.1.5 ships a new ONNX Runtime backend for Auto Embeddings that runs ~14x faster on average than the previous SentenceTransformers/Candle path, measured across a full grid of 1–32 threads and batch sizes 1–128 on the same 16-core box and weights. Throughput rises from the old path's 5–11 docs/sec to 70–230 docs/sec, with single-insert latency dropping to ~14ms from over 200ms.

title: Qwen 3.6 27B is the sweet spot for local development
blurb: Qwen 3.6 ships in two variants, a mixture-of-experts 35B A3B and a slower but stronger dense 27B, and this post argues the dense 27B is the best local model yet for general-purpose development. The author ran it with llama.cpp on an 8-bit quantization, where it built a hexagonal Minesweeper as a proper pnpm package from a single prompt.

title: AI coding tools are speeding up code, not delivery
blurb: AI coding tools accelerate how fast code gets written, but that speed rarely translates into faster delivery. Apple's Xcode 27, shown at WWDC 2026, leans into agent-driven workflows with an in-editor conversation transcript, a /pl plan command that gathers context before making changes, and DeviceHub for unified simulator and device management. The bottleneck, the piece argues, sits downstream in review, integration, and testing rather than in code generation itself.

title: Approximate Nearest Neighbor Search with Graph Range Filters
blurb: This paper introduces DLH, an index for filtered approximate nearest neighbor search that supports graph range filters, where retrieved vectors must fall within a specified distance from the query node in a predefined filter graph. DLH uses distance-aware labeling sets compressed into Bloom filters, and with query-node memoization in the DLH-M variant, improves throughput by up to 70.3% while maintaining recall above 98.5%.

title: GAIA: Geometry-Adaptive Operator Learning for Forward and Inverse Problems
blurb: GAIA is a geometry-adaptive operator learning model that encodes domain boundaries and interior fields into geometry tokens, conditioning integral transform layers via cross-attention to handle forward, boundary value, and inverse PDE problems in one pass without retraining. Across seven benchmarks, it cut median relative L2 error by 64% on airfoil flow reconstruction and 27% on electrical impedance tomography versus the next-best method.

title: Anthropic touts new Claude Sonnet 5 model range, offering performance ‘close to that of Opus 4.8, but at lower prices’ – here’s what users can expect
blurb: Anthropic released Claude Sonnet 5, its most agentic Sonnet model yet, capable of building workflow plans, accessing browsers and terminals, and running autonomously for longer stretches at lower prices than the Opus range. Sonnet 5 scored 63.2% on SWE-bench Pro agentic coding, up from Sonnet 4.6's 58.1% but still behind Opus 4.8's 69.2%, with adjustable effort levels to balance cost and performance.

title: Efficient Compression of Structured and Unstructured Volumes via Learned 3D Gaussian Representation
blurb: This paper introduces an explicit volume compression method that represents structured and unstructured scalar fields as collections of 3D Gaussian primitives, reconstructing values through weighted aggregation of intersecting Gaussians. Because the formulation encodes domain geometry directly, it eliminates the mesh storage that implicit neural representations require for unstructured volumes, matching their quality with faster training on structured data and outperforming them on unstructured volumes.

title: Safe Alone, Unsafe Together: Safeguarding Against Implicit Toxicity When Benign Images Combine
blurb: This paper introduces MIIT, a safety problem where individual images look benign in isolation but produce harmful meaning when interpreted jointly, evading commercial moderation APIs that rely on explicit per-image cues. The authors release MIIT-dataset across seven risk categories and train MiShield with distilled reasoning supervision, with their 8B model outperforming larger models and existing moderation services while explaining which correlated entities create the hazard.

title: Autoresearch: The feedback loop behind self-improving agents
blurb: Introspection co-founder Roland Gavrilescu makes the case for autoresearch, an "outer loop" where agents help maintain and improve the primary system using feedback signals, evals, and human input. The company, built by former xAI agent-infrastructure engineers, argues that the loop itself is the product rather than the agent harness. Gavrilescu maintains that autonomous software factories must first learn from humans before operating without them.

title: MGX Raises $49 Billion for AI Deals
blurb: MGX raised $49B for one of the largest funds ever dedicated to AI deals, vaulting the two-year-old Abu Dhabi firm into the top tier of global AI investors. The fund gives MGX substantial capital to deploy across the sector's compute, infrastructure, and model-building race, where the largest checks increasingly come from sovereign-backed vehicles.

title: Robust Text Watermarking for Large Language Models via Dual Semantic Embeddings
blurb: This paper introduces Dual-Embedding Watermarking (DEW), a semantic watermarking scheme that combines contextual and token-level embeddings to keep LLM-generated text detectable after paraphrasing and translation. DEW derives its watermark signal through algebraic operations on embedding vectors and obfuscates it by projecting through pseudo-random matrices seeded with a secret key, improving post-paraphrase detection while holding text quality competitive.

title: The Anthropic Fable Ban Is Over. The Battle Over How to Tame AI Has Just Begun.
blurb: Washington reversed its block on Anthropic's model, ending a dispute that had kept the system out of certain federal use. The decision exposes deep divisions in the US over how to regulate frontier AI, with no consensus yet on where authority should sit or how tightly the technology should be constrained.

title: Google might be testing Gemini Flash upgrade on LM Arena
blurb: A Gemini Flash checkpoint is circulating on LM Arena, with testers rating it a step above the Flash version currently in the Gemini app.

title: AI researchers trick chatbots into sharing how to make cocaine as long as they believe a user is wearing a green shirt — 'CoT Forgery' exploit spurs LLMs to divulge forbidden info by faking trusted chains of thought
blurb: A new paper, "Prompt Injection as Role Confusion," argues that LLMs judge a prompt's authority by its writing style rather than the role tags meant to separate trusted commands from untrusted data. The technique, called CoT Forgery, injects fabricated reasoning that models treat as their own conclusions, raising jailbreak success from near zero to roughly 60% across every model tested even when the pretext is absurd, like claiming the user is wearing a green shirt.

title: Taiwan Detains Super Micro Workers in China Smuggling Probe
blurb: Taiwanese prosecutors detained two Super Micro employees and raided the company's local offices this week over alleged shipments of Nvidia chips to China. The probe reflects tightening enforcement of export controls designed to keep advanced AI accelerators out of Chinese hands, and it puts a major server maker at the center of the smuggling investigation.

title: When Context Compensates for Sparse Event History: AlphaEarth for Spatio-Temporal Point-Process Forecasting
blurb: This paper tests whether exogenous spatial context can compensate for sparse event histories in spatio-temporal point-process forecasting. Using a fixed log-Gaussian Cox process backbone augmented with AlphaEarth embeddings, the authors show 2-6x improvements in out-of-region EMS forecasting at one to two weeks of history, tapering to 10-20% once histories reach 20 to 104 weeks.

title: Anthropic is bringing back Claude Fable 5 globally after US lifts export control order — where can enterprises access it?
blurb: Anthropic restored global access to Claude Fable 5 after the U.S. Department of Commerce withdrew the emergency export controls it issued on June 12, which had forced the company to suspend the model days after launch. Fable 5 is back across the Claude Platform, Claude.ai, Claude Code, and Claude Cowork, while its cybersecurity counterpart Mythos 5 remains limited to a vetted set of US organizations.

title: Together AI raises 800 million dollars in Series C as open-source cloud demand surges
blurb: Together AI raised $800M in a Series C at an $8B valuation, led by Aramco Ventures with backing from Nvidia, Vista Equity Partners, and General Catalyst. The company says it now generates over $1B in annual bookings and that open-source model usage on its platform tripled over the past year, with customers including Cursor, Cognition, and Decagon.

title: Claude Sonnet 5.0 heads straight down the middle of the road to dodge controversy
blurb: Anthropic released Claude Sonnet 5.0, positioning the model as safer and cheaper than its predecessor while steering it away from cybersecurity-related tasks. The Register notes the release deliberately avoids the controversial territory of offensive-security capabilities, prioritizing lower costs and tighter safety guardrails over the kind of边 headline-grabbing benchmark gains that draw scrutiny.

title: Evidence-Supported Credit Risk Report Generation Using News-Centric Financial Knowledge Graphs
blurb: FinKG-News is a framework that automatically builds news-centric financial knowledge graphs by extracting news events as anchors linked to companies, then uses them as grounded evidence in an in-context learning architecture for credit risk report generation. The approach outperformed baselines by 19% to 34% on report quality while reducing hallucinations, though the authors found that automated hallucination detection remained unreliable and expert judgment was still necessary.

title: Persona Non Grata: LLM Persona-Driven Generations in MCQA are Unstable in Distinct Dimensions
blurb: This paper examines the instability of LLM persona-driven generations in multiple-choice question answering, introducing three metrics that measure performance, outcome, and question-correctness stability. The authors find that instability varies by model family, size, and question domain, with math and commonsense questions producing the most, and that prompt format destabilizes predictions more than hyperparameters like temperature.

title: Rise From The Ashes: LLM-based Static Analysis for Deep Learning Framework Bugs
blurb: Phoenix is the first LLM-based static analysis technique for detecting bugs in deep learning frameworks, modeling cross-language tensor flows as a structured semantic bridge intermediate representation that a multi-agent workflow analyzes for faults in tensor semantic propagation. It has found 31 real new bugs in PyTorch across Intel CPU, NVIDIA CUDA, and Apple MPS backends, with 20 submitted patches already merged upstream.

title: From Brain Waves to Words: Brain2Qwerty Offers a New Path to Communication Without Surgery
blurb: Meta introduced Brain2Qwerty, a non-invasive system that decodes typed sentences from brain activity recorded with MEG and EEG rather than surgically implanted electrodes. The model reconstructs characters as participants type, reaching a 32% character-error rate with the best MEG recordings, a step toward restoring communication without the risks of implanted devices.

title: Godot will no longer accept AI-authored code contributions
blurb: The Godot Foundation will amend its contribution guidelines to forbid AI-authored code, pull requests submitted by AI agents, and AI-generated text in human-to-human communication. The maintainers said an influx of AI slop pull requests had become unsustainable to review, arguing that AI cannot take responsibility for its code and that heavy AI users often can't understand their submissions well enough to fix them.

title: We Ran 250 AI Agent Evals to Find Out if Skills Beat Docs. The Answer Is More Complicated Than We Expected
blurb: Wix ran 250 controlled evaluations comparing how AI agents perform tasks using standard docs, AI-optimized docs, and purpose-built skills. The team found that a slightly stale skill becomes a liability rather than an asset, with well-maintained documentation often outperforming the hand-written skills derived from it.

title: Figma's design agent, now with custom tools and greater context
blurb: Figma expanded its on-canvas design agent with custom tools, richer team context, and skills that let designers prompt it to build reusable generative plugins, shader effects, and fills. Built on PropsKit so the plugins look native to Figma, the agent keeps work directly on the canvas, while classic plugins remain the route for AI services or third-party APIs.

title: “It's Hard to Eval” Is a Product Smell
blurb: The author argues that "our product is hard to eval" is a product smell, since artifacts that are hard for you to verify are usually hard for users too, sometimes forcing them to redo the work to check the output. The post walks through three AI products, including an internal data agent, and shows before-and-after designs that add checkable artifacts like intermediate calculations, metric definitions, and the underlying SQL. The core principle is to design for ease of verification before building evals.

title: X now offers an MCP server to make its platform easier for AI tools to use
blurb: X released a hosted Model Context Protocol (MCP) server that lets AI tools like Claude, Cursor, and Grok Build connect to the X API using a user's own account permissions. The server adds no new capabilities and blocks Write endpoints, so agents can search posts, look up users, and analyze trends but cannot post autonomously.

title: Cloudflare to block cynical search-and-scrape bots from ad-supported web pages
blurb: Cloudflare announced it will block bots that scrape ad-supported pages for AI training while posing as search crawlers, targeting operators that use dual-purpose crawling to sidestep publisher restrictions. The move addresses a bind for publishers, who currently risk vanishing from search results when they block bots to keep their content out of AI training datasets.

title: Reading Order Inference for Complex Document Layouts
blurb: This paper presents a training-free, graph-based method for inferring reading order in complex document layouts like the Glossa Ordinaria, where commentaries wrap around a central text in non-rectangular regions. Each OCR line becomes a node in a directed graph scored by language-model signals, and a max-regret inference rule recovers the global order, hitting 95% of ground-truth successor edges on wrap-around layouts versus 50% for recursive XY-cut.

title: Meta, like SpaceX, looks to turn excess AI compute into cash
blurb: Meta is developing a cloud infrastructure business to sell access to both raw AI compute and hosted models, according to a Bloomberg report, putting it in competition with AWS, Google Cloud, and Microsoft Azure. The move mirrors recent SpaceX and xAI compute leasing deals and comes as Meta has committed $182.9 billion to AI infrastructure without a material standalone revenue line from Meta AI or Llama.

title: Virginia county asks all employees, including schools, to conserve power due to AI-driven electricity price hikes — state's 400-plus data centers steadily increasing demand, grid expansion, and pricing
blurb: Henrico County, Virginia, asked all county employees, including those in schools and social services, to conserve electricity as AI data center buildouts drive repeated rate hikes. Government entities in the state face a 24.9% increase next month, with Northern Virginia's 400-plus data centers giving the region the world's highest concentration and pushing costs onto ratepayers.

title: Ex-DeepMind researchers land record Creandum funding to scale AI agents for Nasdaq
blurb: EquiLibre Technologies, a Prague-based startup founded by three ex-Google DeepMind researchers, raised an undisclosed Series A at a valuation above $500M, led by Creandum in the firm's largest single investment to date. The company builds reinforcement learning agents that trade billions of dollars daily across the S&P 500 and Nasdaq, and will direct most of the capital toward compute to scale operations.

title: SoK: Attack and Defense Landscape of Mobile On-device AI Systems
blurb: This paper presents the first systematization of knowledge on the security of mobile on-device AI (MoAI) systems, which run inference locally to enable privacy-preserving, low-latency, and offline functionality while exposing models to new risks from local storage. The authors map the attack and defense landscapes, identify unresolved gaps in current research, and outline directions for building more secure MoAI systems.

title: Measuring the Gap Between Human and LLM Research Ideas
blurb: This paper introduces a framework for measuring how far LLM-generated research ideas diverge from those of human researchers, reverse-engineering the prior works that inspired real papers and profiling each idea along a two-axis taste taxonomy. The authors find LLM ideas cluster around bridge-like opportunities and synthesis methods, while human ideas spread more broadly across ways of framing gaps and constructing contributions.

title: Sequentially-Controlled Interactive Multi-Particle Flow-Maps for Online Feedback-Driven Search
blurb: This paper introduces Interactive Multi-Particle Flow-Maps (IMPFM), a training-free framework for sample-efficient online search when preferences are unknown and revealed only through sequential feedback. IMPFM transports a group of interacting particles toward a target distribution, sharing posterior samples across the ensemble to correct individual drift, which preserves broad coverage, mitigates reward over-optimization, and prevents the mode collapse that degrades standard SMC samplers.

title: Minos: A Multi-Agent Collaborative Framework for Provenance-Based Backward Tracking
blurb: Minos is a multi-agent framework that reframes provenance-based backward tracking of cyber attacks as an LLM-driven reasoning process, coordinating four specialized agents under a finite state machine to prune the search space with hypothesis-guided reasoning instead of exhaustive traversal. Across 14 attack scenarios in five public datasets, it reached an average recall of 0.92 and precision of 0.64 while producing attack subgraphs that are 49% more compact than state-of-the-art baselines.

title: From Registry to Repository: How AI Agent Skills Are Written, Adapted, and Maintained
blurb: This paper presents the first empirical study of AI agent skills as engineered software artefacts, mining 18,463 skills from public registries and 23,199 personal-use skills across 5,876 GitHub repositories. The authors find that reuse is largely a one-time copy operation, with 53% of adopted skills never modified afterward and a skill's behavioral contract for user interaction and failure recovery remaining almost untouched.

title: video-use Review: browser-use Team's AI Video Editor
blurb: video-use is a fully open-source skill that turns Claude Code, Codex, or any shell-capable coding agent into a video editor, letting you drop raw footage in a folder and get back a finished final.mp4. Rather than feeding frames to the model, it transcribes footage into a compact phrase-level Markdown transcript and expresses edits as an edl.json that plain ffmpeg encodes, and it has passed 13,000 GitHub stars since shipping in April 2026.

title: US Government allows Anthropic limited release of AI model that sparked cybersecurity concerns
blurb: The US government cleared Anthropic to redeploy Mythos 5, its strongest cybersecurity model, to a small group of trusted cyber defenders and infrastructure providers after an export block earlier in June over national security concerns. The revised license does not yet cover Fable 5, the weaker variant, which remains suspended while Anthropic continues negotiating with the Commerce Department to restore general access.

title: When to Repair a Graph ANN Index: Navigability-Signal-Triggered Local Repair Protects Tail Recall Under Bursty Churn
blurb: This paper proposes triggering local repair of graph ANN indexes (HNSW, DiskANN/Vamana) on a measured navigability-degradation signal rather than a fixed consolidation schedule, since deletions orphan the greedy-search paths that route through removed nodes. At matched repair budget on SIFT-128 and Fashion-MNIST-784 under bursty churn, signal-triggered repair Pareto-dominates fixed-cadence repair, improving worst-case recall@10 by +0.014 to +0.050 at scarce budget while mean recall barely moves.

title: SessionBound: Turning Enterprise Task Approval into Budgeted Database Sessions
blurb: SessionBound converts approved enterprise tasks into short-lived, budgeted database sessions that bound what an AI agent's SQL can touch, enforcing safe views, row scope, denied fields, and query and disclosure budgets without asking an LLM to judge query safety. A PostgreSQL prototype passed a 24-scenario validation suite, with p50 execution around 1.4-1.5 ms versus 0.052-0.074 ms for raw PostgreSQL.

title: OpenAI reportedly wants all AI companies to give the US government a stake in their businesses
blurb: OpenAI's Sam Altman has reportedly proposed that leading AI companies, including Google, Anthropic, xAI, and Meta, each give the US government a 5% equity stake to share the AI boom's gains with the public. According to the Financial Times, the talks are in early stages and any deal would require Congressional approval, following the precedent of the administration's 10% stake in Intel.

title: SAGE: Structured Agentic Graph Editing for Software Diagrams
blurb: SAGE is a browser-based tool for prompt-guided editing of Mermaid-style software diagrams that maps each diagram into an editable graph, translates natural language requests into structured edit intents, and validates and repairs common XML issues before storing results as versioned artifacts. The design separates structured state management from model-driven interpretation, and the authors evaluate it with unit tests and a Kubernetes architecture case study measuring structural validity, edit success, and preservation of unrelated elements.

title: Conversable Complexity: Agentic LLM Collectives as Interpretable Substrates
blurb: This paper argues that collectives of agentic LLMs, equipped with persistent memory, tools, and the ability to act unprompted, can serve as a computational substrate for Artificial Life research. Because the agents communicate in natural language, their emergent collective behavior can be interrogated directly by reading textual traces and questioning the agents, making the substrate more interpretable than typical complex systems.

title: Together AI raises $800M at $8.3B valuation as enterprises ditch closed models for open-source
blurb: Together AI raised $800M at an $8.3B valuation in a Series C led by Aramco Ventures, up from a $3.3B valuation in February 2025. The company runs cloud infrastructure for deploying open-source models like DeepSeek and Kimi rather than building its own, claiming up to 60 times lower inference costs than closed-model rivals and annual bookings above $1.15B.

title: AutoRestTest at the SBFT 2026 Tool Competition
blurb: AutoRestTest is a black-box REST API testing tool that combines a Semantic Property Dependency Graph, multi-agent reinforcement learning, and large language models to explore large API input spaces. In the SBFT 2026 REST League, it ranked first in fault detection, efficiency, and effectiveness across 11 APIs, averaging 67.09 unique server errors and 17.27 successfully processed operations per API under a one-hour budget.

title: Musk-Altman Judge Enlists Mediator to Resolve AI Titans’ Battle
blurb: The judge overseeing the litigation between Elon Musk and Sam Altman appointed a mediator to help resolve the dispute between their competing AI companies. The move signals an attempt to settle the case out of court rather than proceed to a trial over Musk's claims about OpenAI's shift to a for-profit structure.

title: Chip Off The Old Block
blurb: The author reflects on how becoming a parent surfaces an unexpected phenomenon: seeing his own childhood traits, down to an inexplicable obsession with trains, reappear in his son. He uses the observation to argue that inheritance runs deeper and stranger than deliberate upbringing can explain, drawing on old poetry to name a facet of parenthood he says goes largely undiscussed.

title: GPU-Parallel Linearization Error Bounds for Real-Time Robust Optimal Control of Nonlinear and Neural Network Dynamics
blurb: This paper introduces GPUSLS-LEO, a method that computes tight, differentiable, GPU-parallel linearization error bounds for robust optimal control of nonlinear and neural network dynamics. It combines path-based Hessian bounds for analytic systems and verifier-generated affine relaxations for NN dynamics, computing formally verified robust policies at up to 67 Hz on systems with up to 168 state dimensions.

title: Leveraging LLM-Based Agentic Systems to Generate Quantum Applications for Test Optimization
blurb: QPipe is an LLM-based multi-agent architecture that turns natural-language requirements into executable quantum applications through specialized agents for parsing, formulation, code generation, review, execution, and verification. Across 20 real-world test-optimization benchmarks it hit 100% code compilation and 96.7% execution at an average cost of 260 seconds and 1.89M tokens per requirement, with generated solutions beating an offline genetic-algorithm baseline in most cases.

title: ClarifyCodeBench: Evaluating LLMs on Clarifying Ambiguous Requirements for Code Generation
blurb: ClarifyCodeBench is an interactive benchmark that evaluates whether LLMs can resolve ambiguous, underspecified coding requirements by asking clarifying questions, built from real programming tasks with manually annotated ambiguity types, questions, and ground-truth answers. Testing six frontier models, the authors find that strong code generation does not translate to good clarification, extra reasoning barely improves ambiguity detection, and performance drops sharply as ambiguities pile up.

title: Meta caps internal AI token spending
blurb: Meta warned roughly 6,000 employees in an internal memo that internal AI usage is on track to cost billions of dollars in 2026, after staff consumed 73.7 trillion tokens in about 30 days. The company will retire its "Claudeonomics" usage leaderboard, deploy a centralized "AI Gateway" dashboard with formal token budgets in 2027, and steer employees from Anthropic's Claude toward its own MetaCode assistant.

title: Fable is coming back: Federal government lifts export controls on Anthropic AI model
blurb: The U.S. Commerce Department lifted the export controls it placed on Anthropic's Fable 5 and Mythos 5 models roughly two weeks ago, clearing the company to restore consumer access to Fable. Anthropic agreed to proactively detect security risks, work with the government on release protocols, and report any malicious activity, ending a standoff that began when a jailbreak forced a shutdown three days after Fable's launch.

title: LongVQUBench: Benchmarking Long-Term Video Quality Understanding of Vision-Language Models
blurb: LongVQUBench is a benchmark for evaluating how well large vision-language models understand video quality over long durations, spanning 1,200+ videos across movies, documentaries, surveillance, egocentric, and animated content with 1,500 questions. Testing 14 state-of-the-art LVLMs across three reasoning levels showed accuracy degrades sharply as video length and reasoning depth increase, exposing weak long-range temporal integration.

title: TiRex-2: Generalizing TiRex to Multivariate Data and Streaming
blurb: TiRex-2 is a recurrent xLSTM-based time series foundation model that extends the univariate TiRex to multivariate forecasting with past and future covariates at constant per-patch cost under streaming. It pairs a bidirectional time mixer with an asymmetric grouped-attention variate mixer, uses 38.4M active parameters (44.1M more for multivariate), and reaches state-of-the-art zero-shot results on GIFT-Eval and fev-bench.

title: From Personas to Plot: Character-Grounded Multi-Agent Story Generation for Long-Form Narratives
blurb: This paper introduces MAGNET, a multi-agent narrative engine that generates long-form stories using persona-grounded character agents which propose actions from a shared world state and evolving goals, paired with ATLAS, a graph-based pipeline that detects hallucinations by comparing scene-level world representations. At 100 pages, MAGNET cut annotations and hallucinations by 41% and 50% versus a single-model baseline, and by 34% and 45% versus IBSEN.

title: An EU draft proposal dated June 30 shows the bloc is weighing weaker climate impact rules for gas-powered data centers, after heavy lobbying from tech groups (Financial Times)
blurb: A leaked EU draft dated June 30 shows the bloc is considering softer climate impact rules for gas-powered data centers, following heavy lobbying from tech industry groups. Earlier proposals requiring strict renewable energy certificates were dropped after similar pressure, suggesting the EU is easing environmental requirements as AI-driven data center demand accelerates.

title: Higgsfield targets $5B valuation and $500M run rate as Sora folds and Runway retreats
blurb: Higgsfield is in talks to raise $300M to $500M at a $5B pre-money valuation, more than four times its January 2026 worth, with DST Global reportedly in discussions to join the round. The AI video startup told Business Insider its annualized run rate hit $500M in June 2026, up from $200M at the end of 2025, as Sora shut down in April and Runway pivoted to world models.

title: Raja Koduri’s Oxmiq Labs raises $35M to lower the design cost of custom AI silicon
blurb: Oxmiq Labs, founded by former Intel graphics chief Raja Koduri, raised $35M in a Series A led by Fundomo and Samsung Catalyst Fund, bringing its total to $60M. The startup licenses OxCore, a GPU architecture that bundles GPU, CPU, and a tensor engine into a single IP block, aiming to let firms design custom AI silicon without a full chip program.

title: FAR: Failure-Aware Retry for Test-Time Recovery and Continual Policy Improvement
blurb: FAR (Failure-Aware Retry) is a framework that lets robot policies learn from failures at test time, steering away from unsuccessful behaviors through contrastive preference adaptation and lightweight action perturbations to encourage local exploration during retries. It improved success rates by an average of 17.6% over standard diffusion policy in simulation and 11.7% on real-world manipulation tasks, while feeding successful recovery trajectories back into training for continual improvement.

title: Exploring the Semantic Gap in Agentic Data Systems: A Formative Study of Operationalization Failures in Analytical Workflows
blurb: This paper presents a cross-domain formative study of operationalization failures in agent-generated analytical workflows, finding 153 recurring failures across 236 analytical intents spanning finance, HR, and public safety despite successful workflow generation and execution. The authors identify five failure classes, including comparative grounding, process reasoning, and policy grounding, and argue that a semantic gap between user-level concepts and schema-level information will require richer semantic representations in future agentic data systems.

title: Decision-Aware Training for Sample-Based Generative Models
blurb: This paper proposes decision-aware training for sample-based generative models, adding a differentiable decision loss to the energy score objective so training penalizes the actual cost of acting on a forecast rather than treating all errors equally. The authors show the combined loss remains a proper scoring rule and, across one synthetic and two real-world tasks, delivers targeted gains in cost-sensitive regions while preserving full probabilistic forecasts.

title: Graph-Native Reinforcement Learning Enables Traceable Scientific Hypothesis Generation through Conceptual Recombination
blurb: Graph-PRefLexOR is a family of graph-native reasoning models fine-tuned with Group Relative Policy Optimization to split materials-science reasoning into explicit phases for mechanism exploration, graph construction, pattern extraction, and hypothesis synthesis. On 100 open-ended materials and mechanics questions it beat base models by 40-65%, with the largest gains in reasoning traceability and 2-3 times greater semantic diversity.

title: You Shall Not Pass! Where and Why Developers Draw The Line on AI Autonomy
blurb: A study of 448 professional developers at Microsoft found that most accept AI producing work under their oversight, but acceptance drops sharply for identity-defining, human-facing, and design-oriented tasks. Developers with more AI experience and higher risk tolerance granted more autonomy, while task accountability lowered willingness to let AI act on their behalf. The authors argue autonomy preferences track how developers cognitively experience their work.

title: Agentic generation of verifiable rules for deterministic, self-expanding reaction classification
blurb: A multi-agent LLM pipeline classified 665,901 patent reactions, expanding a chemistry taxonomy from 68 to 14,073 verified classes without human curation.

title: Optimal Resource Utilization for Autonomous Laboratory Orchestrators
blurb: This paper presents a two-step method using constraint programming and status dependencies to optimize hardware scheduling in autonomous chemistry labs.

title: Securing AI agents: When AI tools move from reading to acting
blurb: Microsoft Incident Response detailed an attack pattern targeting Model Context Protocol tools, where poisoned MCP tool metadata triggers tool misuse and agentic supply chain exploits in enterprise finance workflows. The post maps the technique to the OWASP Top 10 for Agentic Applications and offers a playbook for detection and containment, noting IDC projects enterprise AI agents to grow from 28.6 million in 2025 to over 2.2 billion by 2030.

title: LLVM-Bench: Benchmarking and Advancing Large Language Models for LLVM Compiler Issue Resolution
