blurb: Cloudflare announced the Monetization Gateway, an edge engine that lets customers charge for any protected resource, including web pages, datasets, APIs, and MCP tools. It offers a single control plane for payment policies and access controls while shielding origins from payment volume, with settlement in stablecoins over the x402 protocol backed by a 25-member foundation.

title: How we keep GPUs reliable across Databricks AI
blurb: Databricks AI engineers detailed how they keep GPUs reliable across training workloads that run at massive scale every week, opening a series with the failure modes, workloads, and multi-stage health checks that catch problems early. They sort most GPU failures into three classes: crashed jobs, silent slowdowns, and numerical corruption, noting that a crashed run surfaces almost everything as the same NCCL watchdog timeout that reveals nothing about root cause. The hard cases are jobs that complete with wrong numbers or run degraded for hours before anyone notices.

title: Fixing a kubelet memory leak in Kubernetes 1.36
blurb: The author traced a kubelet memory leak in Kubernetes 1.36 that surfaced on a 2 GiB DOKS node, where the kubelet process grew unbounded while pod-level metrics stayed flat. Dumping the running process's heap with Go pprof pinned the growth on leaked `context.cancelCtx` objects piling up from cancel-context propagation. The writeup is a clean walkthrough of profiling a core node component in place, useful for anyone debugging memory pressure that `kubectl top` can't explain.

title: Stochastic Connectivity as the Foundation of a Runtime Model for Microservice Availability Analysis
blurb: This paper proposes a formal model that estimates microservice endpoint availability from traces and deployment metadata via Monte Carlo simulation, cutting reliance on costly chaos experiments.

title: What do AI observability tools actually do?
blurb: The author argues that today's AI observability tools are built for yesterday's problems, since AI degrades through drift and hallucination rather than clean error codes and predictable execution paths. The current landscape leans on offline evals, LLM-as-a-judge scoring, and human graders that are static, backward-looking, and hard to scale. OpenTelemetry and LLM tracing add early runtime visibility, but the piece contends teams need to observe systems live in production, not just score outputs after the fact.

title: Cloudflare to block cynical search-and-scrape bots from ad-supported web pages
blurb: Cloudflare will let publishers block crawlers that scrape ad-supported pages for AI training while still allowing legitimate search indexing, addressing bots that currently gather data for both purposes. Marketers relying on organic search should see clearer separation between search visibility and AI-training exposure, letting them protect proprietary content without vanishing from Google or Bing results.

title: Papa Johns Surveillance-Based Advertising
blurb: Papa Johns worked with NBCUniversal, Instacart, and Carat to build a custom audience of Instacart shoppers, then predicted which days those consumers run low on grocery staples and served them QR-code streaming ads reading "Empty fridge?" and "Light on groceries?" The campaign tunes creative to purchase history, and to avoid seeming "too creepy" the team deliberately targets imperfectly, mirroring the misdirection Target used with its pregnancy-prediction model in 2012.

title: Google adds Channel Diagnostics to Performance Max
blurb: Google added Channel Diagnostics to Performance Max, giving advertisers a centralized view under Insights & Reports of missing or disapproved assets that limit campaign eligibility across Search, Display, YouTube, Discover, Gmail, and Maps. The feature targets PMax's longstanding visibility gap, letting advertisers pinpoint which headlines, descriptions, or images need fixing without manually auditing each asset group.

title: What Meta’s broad targeting teaches us about optimization signals
blurb: On Meta, the conversion event you optimize for now drives who the algorithm targets and how it spends, often more than audience settings do. Broad targeting works not because targeting stopped mattering but because Meta's model reads more purchase signals than any advertiser can, so feeding it the right event is the real lever. The catch: broad-targeting logic that wins in purchase-optimized conversion campaigns fails in traffic campaigns, where the signal Meta receives points at clicks rather than buyers.

title: TikTok Launches Ad Tool for Branded Mini Dramas
blurb: TikTok launched Growth Max for Mini Dramas, an ad tool that lets brands promote episodic microdrama content and monetize it through in-episode ads and direct viewer payments. The company said Growth Max campaigns drove a 52% lift in incremental reach with minimal audience overlap, arriving as US microdrama viewers are forecast to grow 130% to 5 million in 2026.

title: Adobe is Buying Topaz Labs, the AI Video Enhancer
blurb: Adobe agreed to acquire Topaz Labs, the Emmy-winning maker of AI upscaling, restoration, and on-device enhancement tools, for an undisclosed price. The company plans to fold Topaz's models into Firefly and Creative Cloud apps like Photoshop, Lightroom, and Premiere, betting that creators blending real and AI-generated footage will pay for tools that hide the seams.

title: The Rise and Fall of Google's Privacy Sandbox
blurb: Google retired most Privacy Sandbox APIs in October 2025, ending nearly five years of work on a privacy-preserving alternative to cross-site tracking, and a new longitudinal study of the top 10,000 sites finds adoption had already stagnated to a handful of actors well before the announcement. With only the CHIPS partitioned-cookie API showing any staying power, marketers are left without a standardized replacement for interest-based targeting as third-party cookies wind down.

title: Google Search now sends searchers directly to publisher-hosted AMP pages
blurb: Google Search now routes users directly to publisher-hosted AMP pages instead of Google-cached versions shown in the AMP viewer, while rankings in Search and Discover stay unchanged. Publishers get simpler analytics and tracking on their own domain, and lower maintenance overhead, though the move mostly formalizes AMP's diminished role after years of losing its preferential search treatment.

title: Google tests AI-generated summaries in Search ads
blurb: Google is testing AI-generated summaries beneath Search ads, adding a disclaimer that its AI responses are independent and can make mistakes. The move hands Google, not the advertiser, control over which ad details get surfaced, raising fresh questions about brand messaging, copy accuracy, and click-through performance if the experiment expands beyond its current small test.

title: ChatGPT Thinking mode changes which brands get cited
blurb: ChatGPT's high-reasoning Thinking mode behaves like a separate search surface, with only 25.6% of cited domains overlapping with minimal reasoning across 100 prompts, per a Semrush analysis with Kevin Indig. Reddit and review sites lost share while documentation and academic sources gained, meaning brands optimizing for AI visibility now need to win two distinct citation environments rather than one.

title: U.S. state data privacy laws: What you need to know
blurb: A growing patchwork of U.S. state privacy laws now governs consumer data across red and blue states alike, from California and Colorado to newly added Louisiana and Vermont, each with different scope, definitions, and opt-out requirements. California's Delete Request and Opt-Out Platform launched January 1, letting residents wipe their data from 500+ registered brokers in one request, with brokers required to start processing deletions by August 1.

title: Podcasting Platform Riverside Expands Into Publishing and Distribution
blurb: Riverside expanded beyond recording and editing into publishing, rolling out AI-generated newsletters, social scheduling, and a rebuilt studio, and rebranded from riverside.fm to riverside.com. The pitch to creators is a team of AI agents that turns a finished recording into a rough cut, social clips, a newsletter draft, and scheduled posts, collapsing the distribution work that eats most of their time.

title: X launches livestream studio to streamline live broadcasts
blurb: X launched a redesigned livestreaming studio on desktop, giving live video creators more production controls and a streamlined workflow for broadcasting on the platform. The upgrade positions X to compete for creators splitting time across Twitch, YouTube Live, and LinkedIn Live, and marketers running live product demos or events now have a native option with built-in reach to X's existing audience.

title: GraphRAG: What entity-first retrieval means for SEO
blurb: GraphRAG extends retrieval-augmented generation with a knowledge graph, replacing flat text chunks with a map of entities and the relationships between them, which lets AI systems follow verified paths instead of inferring answers and cite brands with fewer hallucinations. Originating from Microsoft Research in 2024, the shift means SEO now hinges on making your brand machine-readable as connected entities, defining products, people, and certifications and the relationships linking them, rather than optimizing isolated pages for keywords.

title: X is making a fresh push for live video with new creator payouts
blurb: X launched Live Studio, a livestreaming command center in Creator Studio, and allocated $1M to pay creators who go live in the upcoming cycle. Streaming requires a $3-per-month X Premium subscription and remains a small part of the platform, so the payout pool is a bid to build supply before X can prove its infrastructure holds up under scale.

title: How campaign structure shapes Google Ads performance
blurb: Google Ads campaign structure determines how much data Smart Bidding pools per campaign, and over-segmentation is a common performance killer. Smart Bidding strategies like Target CPA and Target ROAS need roughly 30 to 50 conversions per campaign per month to exit the learning phase, so an account split into 12 category campaigns averaging 8-12 conversions each never stabilizes. Consolidating campaigns concentrates the signal, which lets Google's automation optimize on volume instead of scattered fragments.

title: Meta will charge for access to advanced AI features
blurb: Meta confirmed it will charge for advanced AI features and premium options, including device support for its smart glasses.

title: Why TikTok deserves a place in your SEO strategy
blurb: Nearly half of U.S. consumers used TikTok as a search engine in 2026, up from 41% in 2024, per Adobe, and its new discovery surfaces (Local Feed, AI summaries, creator reviews, TikTok Shop) now sit inside the buying journey. TikTok SEO has moved past hashtags and trending sounds to spoken-topic clarity, on-screen text, location context, and creator reviews. Marketers optimizing only for Google are ceding the top of visual, trust-driven searches to competitors who show up where the query actually starts.

title: How ChatGPT Actually Picks Sources (I Read the Network Traffic, Not the Outputs)
blurb: An analyst read two days of ChatGPT's raw network traffic to document how it actually picks sources, pulling the engine's own internal labels like result_source (serp, labrador, bright, oxylabs) and turn_use_case rather than inferring machinery from finished answers. The structural findings hold because a field only has to appear once to be real, but the author flags all percentages as directional, drawn from one logged-in Pro account and roughly 1,240 source records across mostly SaaS queries.

title: Google tests AI-generated summaries in Search ads
blurb: Google is testing AI-generated summaries beneath its Search ads, displaying content Google considers relevant rather than the advertiser's own copy, with a disclaimer noting the responses can be wrong. For PMs at advertisers, the experiment signals eroding control over paid messaging, raising open questions about brand accuracy and click-through performance if the feature moves past its current small test.

title: Europe’s MiCA crypto regime is fully in force: Here’s who wins and loses
blurb: MiCA's final transition period ended July 1, and only 244 of the 3,000-plus crypto firms previously operating under national EU regimes have secured Crypto-Asset Service Provider authorization, with Binance the most notable to miss the deadline after withdrawing its Greek application. The concentration cushions the impact, as Kaiko data shows MiCA-licensed exchanges already account for roughly 83% of European trading volume.

title: French banking giant Crédit Agricole rolls out euro stablecoin, EURXT
blurb: Crédit Agricole, France's second-largest bank, introduced EURXT, a MiCA-compliant euro stablecoin issued on Ethereum by its Caceis asset-servicing arm and backed 1:1 by euro reserves. The token launched with 20M in circulation, trailing Circle's EURC at 378M and SocGen's EURCV at 124M, and has already settled a subscription into a tokenized Amundi money market fund.

title: Tokenized Google stock inflated 7,700% in rare DeFi lending exploit
blurb: Edel Finance halted its lending protocol after an attacker manipulated the exchange rate between wGOOGLx and its underlying tokenized Google share GOOGLx, inflating the wrapped collateral to roughly 78 times its true value and borrowing against it for about $403,000 in bad debt. Chainlink oracles reported Alphabet's ~$357 share price correctly, so the flaw was in the wrapping mechanism, not the price feed; Edel will absorb depositor losses and ship a redesigned v2.

title: Bernstein sees 203% upside for Circle even as new stablecoin rival OUSD debuts
blurb: Bernstein reaffirmed its Outperform rating and $190 price target on Circle, implying 203% upside, a day after CRCL closed down 17.5% on the debut of Open USD, a stablecoin backed by 140+ companies including Visa, Stripe, Mastercard, BlackRock, and Coinbase. The firm argued USDC's $5.3T in H1 2026 volume, up roughly 140% year-over-year, insulates Circle even as its share of the stablecoin base sits near 28%.

title: Ripple, Coinbase among top donors in crypto’s $189 million election spending: report
blurb: Crypto companies have contributed $189 million to the 2026 US midterms so far, or 37% of all disclosed corporate political spending, with Ripple ($49.6M), Crypto.com ($38.6M), and Coinbase ($35.2M) leading the sector, according to a Public Citizen analysis of FEC data. Most of the money flowed to the crypto-focused Fairshake super PAC, which took $82.6 million, and the Trump-backing MAGA Inc., which took $56.2 million.

title: Robinhood Chain goes live on mainnet alongside 24/7 tokenized stocks, Lighter perps and planned crypto agentic trading
blurb: Robinhood Chain, a Layer 2 built on Arbitrum's tech stack, went live on public mainnet with Uniswap among its day-one partners. The launch pairs the chain with 24/7 tokenized stock trading, Lighter-powered perpetuals, and planned crypto agentic trading, extending Robinhood's push to move its brokerage products onchain.

title: EthLabs launches as Ethereum undergoes its biggest leadership transition in years
blurb: EthLabs launched as a nonprofit Ethereum research organization founded by five former Ethereum Foundation researchers, arriving one day before major Foundation layoffs and days after co-executive director Hsiao-Wei Wang resigned. The launch caps a turnover wave that has seen at least nine prominent Foundation members depart since January, with EthLabs positioning itself around real-world adoption as the Foundation narrows its focus.

title: All-out Attack: Optimal Block Withholding Under Pay-Per-Share Scheme
blurb: A new paper analyzes Block Withholding attacks under Pay-Per-Share and Full-PPS mining pool schemes, proving these payout mechanisms are not incentive compatible despite prior claims. The optimal strategy, dubbed the All-out Attack, points all adversarial hashpower at a victim pool, submits only partial shares, and withholds every valid block. After the first difficulty adjustment, the attacker gains α/(1-α) relative to pre-adjustment rates while the pool operator pays out-of-pocket for shares it never gets blocks for.

title: Symbiotic officially pivots to collateral markets with Core V2 launch
blurb: Symbiotic launched Core V2, repositioning the restaking protocol as shared collateral infrastructure rather than a single-purpose security layer. The upgrade lets one collateral base back multiple DeFi use cases including insurance, credit markets, and tokenized real-world assets, without requiring separate deposits for each application.

title: Crypto hack theft falls 7% in June to $76 million as Humanity Protocol tops list: PeckShield
blurb: Crypto exploits totaled $75.9 million across 40 incidents in June, down 7.1% from May's $81.7 million, with the Humanity Protocol private-key breach topping the list at $31 million, according to PeckShield. PeckShield flagged that the Humanity Protocol attacker laundered funds commingled with the Kelp DAO exploiter, raising the possibility that a single threat actor is behind both.

title: Solana launches onchain governance and sets entry fee at 100,000 SOL staked
blurb: Solana activated Solana Governance Proposals, a formal on-chain governance system that gives validators and their delegators a stake-weighted, recorded vote on the network's direction for the first time. Opening a proposal requires at least 100,000 SOL ($7.7M) staked, 15% active-stake support to reach a vote, and a two-thirds supermajority to pass, with staker sovereignty letting delegators override their validator's choice.

title: Announcing the Monetization Gateway: charge for any resource behind Cloudflare via x402
blurb: Cloudflare announced the Monetization Gateway, an engine that lets its customers charge for any protected asset, including web pages, datasets, APIs, and MCP tools. Payments settle in stablecoins over x402, the open protocol Cloudflare is building with more than 25 partners via the x402 Foundation, with verification and enforcement handled at the edge to shield origins from high payment volumes.

title: dYdX Launches Arcus, a DEX Pairing Stock Tokens With Perpetuals on Robinhood Chain
blurb: dYdX Labs launched Arcus, a decentralized exchange built with Robinhood Crypto that pairs tokenized stock trading with perpetual futures on Robinhood Chain, the EVM-compatible L2 Robinhood opened publicly the same day. Spot trading across 95 stock tokens is live for around-the-clock equities exposure, while perpetuals covering 35 real-world-asset markets remain waitlisted, and dYdX Chain v4 keeps operating alongside it.

title: ‘We are bullish on Morpho’: Standard Chartered sees token at $60 by 2030, outperforming bitcoin and ether
blurb: Standard Chartered initiated coverage of Morpho with an end-2030 price target of $60 for the MORPHO token, a trajectory it says would outperform both bitcoin and ether. The bank anchored the forecast on Morpho's lending markets and vault infrastructure, which currently hold about $5.5B and $4.3B in deposits respectively, and on a projected 37x increase in DeFi assets by 2030.

title: Ethereum gets a new nonprofit focused on institutional adoption
blurb: Ethereum Institutional launched as an independent nonprofit to give banks, asset managers, and enterprises a neutral point of contact as they evaluate Ethereum for tokenization, stablecoins, and other onchain financial infrastructure. The group is led by David Walsh, who ran the Ethereum Foundation's enterprise efforts, and arrives as the Foundation narrows its scope to core-protocol stewardship while independent bodies like EthLabs absorb ecosystem functions.

title: No Country for Old Privacy: The Evolving Challenges of Anonymity in Bitcoin
blurb: A longitudinal study measured on-chain adoption of second-generation Bitcoin anonymization protocols, identifying 5.94M CoinJoin and 23.3M CoinSwap transactions via heuristic filters. Detectable usage of CoinJoin, CoinSwap, CoinShuffle, and Stealth Addresses stayed below 1% of all transactions and dropped sharply after major regulatory events, with no convergence on a common Stealth Address standard. The authors read the decline as privacy-seeking users migrating to less detectable methods rather than abandoning privacy.

title: Binance says some European clients may be affected after pulling Greek license bid
blurb: Binance withdrew its bid for a bloc-wide MiCA license through Greek authorities and warned that some EU clients will be affected ahead of the July 1 compliance deadline. The exchange now plans to seek authorization in France, where approval is unlikely before the cutoff, leaving customers in Poland, Italy, Spain, and France instructed on how to withdraw funds.

title: Nansen: about two-thirds of Trump's memecoin investors are currently in the red, and 85% of World Liberty's $WLFI buyers on the secondary market are underwater (Wall Street Journal)
blurb: Nansen data shows roughly two-thirds of investors in Trump's TRUMP memecoin are sitting on losses, while 85% of buyers who acquired World Liberty Financial's $WLFI on the secondary market are underwater. The figures underscore how much of the value in the president-linked tokens accrued to insiders and early allocators rather than retail buyers entering after launch.

title: Solana Launches Onchain Governance With Stake-Weighted Voting for Validators
blurb: Solana Foundation launched onchain governance, letting validators propose and cast stake-weighted votes on protocol decisions through Solana Governance Proposals verified by Merkle proof. Any validator with 100,000 SOL delegated can open a proposal, votes go live once they clear 15% of cluster stake, and delegators can override their validator's vote using their own stake weight.

title: Citi slashes 12-month bitcoin, ether targets as ETF flows dry up
blurb: Citi cut its 12-month bitcoin target to $82,000 from $112,000 and its ether target to $2,240 from $3,175, after scrapping its ETF inflow forecast to assume zero net flows over the next year. The bank cited stalled US market-structure legislation and record June ETF outflows of $4B, the largest monthly withdrawal since the funds launched in 2024.

title: Ethereum Institutional launch draws support from across the Ethereum ecosystem
blurb: Ethereum Institutional launched Wednesday, an independent initiative focused on institutional education, advocacy, and strategic communications, drawing endorsements from Standard Chartered, Etherealize, Aztec Labs, Spark, and Bitwise. The effort arrives alongside EthLabs and the Ethereum Foundation's push to hand adoption and ecosystem growth to multiple independent organizations rather than a single entity, positioning Ethereum for tokenized assets and financial infrastructure.

title: Forward Industries jumps 17% after expanding Solana treasury to 7.55 million SOL
blurb: Forward Industries stock rose 17% after the company expanded its Solana treasury to 7.55 million SOL, more than the next three largest publicly traded Solana treasury firms combined. The position deepens the corporate-treasury bid for SOL, concentrating a large share of institutional Solana holdings in a single Nasdaq-listed vehicle.

title: Kalshi and Polymarket’s combined volume surges 75% to $45 billion in June amid World Cup fever
blurb: Kalshi and Polymarket's combined trading volume rose 75% to $45 billion in June, driven by heavy activity around the FIFA World Cup and other event markets. Kalshi led the gain, growing 87.4% month-over-month to $31.5 billion from $16.81 billion, widening its lead over rival prediction-market venues.

title: Europe is closing the door on offshore crypto, but it’s leaving the riskiest window open
blurb: The EU's July 1 MiCA deadline forces unlicensed crypto asset service providers to wind down spot operations, but the crackdown excludes derivatives, leaving perpetual futures outside MiCA's jurisdiction. That carve-out routes European users toward unauthorized offshore perp platforms like Hyperliquid and Aster offering 50x to 200x leverage, even as ESMA has flagged perps as likely CFDs subject to existing leverage limits and risk warnings.

title: Avalanche Treasury stock plunges 73% since debut as company’s AVAX holdings lose value
blurb: Avalanche Treasury's stock has fallen 73% since its debut, tracking a sharp decline in the value of the AVAX holdings that back the company's balance sheet. Management flagged "substantial doubt" about its ability to continue operations at the end of the first quarter, underscoring the risk in leveraged digital-asset treasury vehicles.

title: FBI Director Kash Patel disclosed six-figure Strategy stock purchase months after trade: report
blurb: FBI Director Kash Patel disclosed a six-figure purchase of Strategy stock in a federal ethics filing submitted months after the trade executed, according to a report. The delayed disclosure adds a compliance question around a senior official's exposure to a company holding roughly 600,000 BTC on its balance sheet, whose equity trades largely as a leveraged proxy for bitcoin.

title: Taiko fully restores cross-chain bridge just 10 days after a $1.7 million hack
blurb: Taiko reopened its Ethereum L2 cross-chain bridge 10 days after a June 22 exploit drained roughly $1.7M from its bridge and ERC20 Vault contracts via a compromised SGX signing key exposed on GitHub. The protocol restored 1:1 reserves, made all affected users whole, and passed an independent security review, while the TAIKO token climbed as much as 136% in recent trading.

title: FBI Director Kash Patel caught sleeping on required disclosure of six-figure MSTR investment: Report
blurb: FBI Director Kash Patel failed to disclose a purchase of between $100,001 and $250,000 in Strategy (MSTR) shares for more than six months, well past the STOCK Act's 45-day window, according to a NOTUS report. The delay drew scrutiny because Strategy is the largest public BTC holder and has done millions in business with the Justice Department, reviving calls to bar federal officials from trading individual stocks.

title: Ark Invest scoops up $18 million in Circle shares as stock dips 41% over past month
blurb: Ark Invest bought roughly $18M in Circle shares as the stablecoin issuer's stock fell 41% over the past month, including a near-18% drop Tuesday tied to the launch of rival stablecoin project OUSD. The purchase adds to Ark's position in USDC's issuer even as competition and a sliding share price weigh on Circle's post-IPO valuation.

title: ‘We desperately need legislation that includes an agreement on ethics:’ Trump financial filing adds urgency to crypto bill negotiations
blurb: Trump's latest financial disclosure revealed millions of dollars in crypto-related income, intensifying pressure on lawmakers to write ethics restrictions into the pending crypto market-structure bill. Negotiators now face a sharper conflict-of-interest question, with critics arguing the legislation cannot advance without provisions limiting how sitting officials profit from the assets it governs.

title: Venice AI raises $65 million Series A at $1 billion equity valuation led by Dragonfly
blurb: Venice AI, the crypto-AI startup founded by Erik Voorhees, raised $65 million in a Series A at a $1 billion equity valuation led by Dragonfly. The round marks one of the larger raises for a private, uncensored inference platform that lets users pay for AI access with its VVV token rather than routing prompts through centralized providers.

title: ‘An honest, neutral counterpart:’ Bitmine, Sharplink and Joe Lubin back new Ethereum Institutional non-profit
blurb: Bitmine, SharpLink, and Consensys CEO Joseph Lubin are backing a new nonprofit called the Ethereum Institutional, positioning it as a neutral counterpart to advocate for ETH among corporate treasuries and traditional finance. The group joins a growing list of Ethereum-focused entities courting institutional capital, following the wave of public companies that have added ETH to their balance sheets this year.

title: Europe’s MiCA crypto regime is fully in force: Here’s who wins and loses
blurb: MiCA's final transition period ended July 1, bringing crypto firms across the EU under a single licensing regime, with only 244 of the roughly 3,000 companies previously operating under national rules now authorized as CASPs. Binance is the highest-profile firm to miss the deadline after withdrawing its Greek application, though Kaiko data shows MiCA-licensed exchanges already account for about 83% of European trading volume.

title: Bernstein sees 203% upside for Circle even as new stablecoin rival OUSD debuts
blurb: Bernstein reaffirmed its Outperform rating and $190 price target on Circle, implying 203% upside, a day after CRCL closed down 17.5% on the debut of Open USD, a stablecoin backed by 140-plus firms including Visa, Stripe, Mastercard, BlackRock, and Coinbase. Bernstein argues the OUSD coalition validates the category without immediately threatening USDC, which processed $5.3T in the first half of 2026 and lifted its share of transaction volume to roughly 60%.

title: Tokenized Google stock inflated 7,700% in rare DeFi lending exploit
blurb: Edel Finance halted its lending protocol after an attacker manipulated the wGOOGLx-to-GOOGLx wrapping mechanism to inflate tokenized Google stock collateral to roughly 78 times its true price, borrowing against it and leaving about $403,000 in bad debt. The flaw was not in Chainlink's price feeds, which correctly reported Alphabet's ~$357 share price, but in the conversion logic, exposing a distinct risk vector as tokenized equities move onchain.

title: French banking giant Crédit Agricole rolls out euro stablecoin, EURXT
blurb: Crédit Agricole introduced EURXT, a euro-pegged, MiCA-compliant stablecoin issued on Ethereum by its asset-servicing arm Caceis, debuting with 20 million tokens backed 1:1 by euro reserves. The launch puts France's second-largest bank into a euro-stablecoin market already contested by Circle's EURC and SocGen's EURCV, ahead of a rival token planned by the 37-bank Qivalis group later this year.

title: Ripple, Coinbase among top donors in crypto’s $189 million election spending: report
blurb: Crypto companies have contributed $189 million to influence the 2026 US midterms, or 37% of all disclosed corporate political spending this cycle, with Ripple ($49.6M), Crypto.com ($38.6M), and Coinbase ($35.2M) leading the sector, according to a Public Citizen analysis of FEC data. Most of the money flowed to Fairshake, a sector-focused super PAC that took in $82.6 million, and the Trump-backing MAGA Inc. at $56.2 million.

title: EthLabs launches as Ethereum undergoes its biggest leadership transition in years
blurb: EthLabs, a new nonprofit led by five former Ethereum Foundation researchers, launched to advance Ethereum's technical roadmap with a sharper focus on real-world adoption, one day before major Foundation layoffs and days after co-executive director Hsiao-Wei Wang resigned. At least nine prominent Foundation members have departed since January, and the split signals a governance realignment as Ethereum shifts from infrastructure build-out toward mainstream deployment.

title: Symbiotic officially pivots to collateral markets with Core V2 launch
blurb: Symbiotic launched Core V2, formally repositioning the protocol from restaking toward shared collateral infrastructure that multiple DeFi applications can draw on. The upgrade lets a single pool of collateral back use cases including insurance, credit, and tokenized real-world assets, positioning Symbiotic as a settlement layer for onchain risk rather than a single-purpose staking primitive.

title: Ethereum Institutional launch draws support from across the Ethereum ecosystem
blurb: Ethereum Institutional launched Wednesday to broad backing from across the ecosystem, with Standard Chartered, Etherealize, Aztec Labs, Spark, and Bitwise framing it as a push to accelerate institutional adoption and cement Ethereum as the base layer for tokenized assets. The effort focuses on education, advocacy, and communications with large financial institutions, and arrives alongside EthLabs and the Ethereum Foundation's move to hand adoption work to independent organizations.

title: Evidence-Supported Credit Risk Report Generation Using News-Centric Financial Knowledge Graphs
blurb: This paper introduces FinKG-News, a framework that builds company-centric financial knowledge graphs from news events, then uses them as grounded evidence for an in-context learning system that generates credit risk reports across three financial dimensions. The authors report 19%-34% quality gains over baselines with fewer hallucinations, but note that automated hallucination detection and quality scoring stayed unreliable, leaving expert judgment essential for any production credit-analysis use.

title: Robinhood Chain goes live on mainnet alongside 24/7 tokenized stocks, Lighter perps and planned crypto agentic trading
blurb: Robinhood Chain, a Layer 2 built on Arbitrum's tech stack, went live on public mainnet with Uniswap among its day-one partners, launching alongside 24/7 tokenized stocks and Lighter-powered perpetuals. Robinhood also plans to add agentic crypto trading on the chain, moving the brokerage toward running its own settlement layer rather than routing order flow through external venues.

title: ‘We are bullish on Morpho’: Standard Chartered sees token at $60 by 2030, outperforming bitcoin and ether
blurb: Standard Chartered initiated coverage of Morpho on Wednesday with an end-2030 price target of $60 for the MORPHO token, a trajectory it says would outperform both bitcoin and ether. The bank's bull case rests on Morpho Vaults pulling in TradFi capital through custody integrations with Fireblocks, Anchorage, and Taurus, positioning the second-largest DeFi lending protocol to scale with a projected 37x increase in on-chain assets by 2030.

title: Ethereum gets a new nonprofit focused on institutional adoption
blurb: Ethereum Institutional, a new independent nonprofit led by former Ethereum Foundation enterprise head David Walsh, launched to give banks and asset managers a neutral point of contact as they evaluate the chain for tokenization, stablecoins, and onchain financial infrastructure. The launch reflects the Ethereum Foundation narrowing its remit to core protocol stewardship, with separate groups like EthLabs now absorbing institutional outreach and ecosystem development.

title: Ex-DeepMind researchers land record Creandum funding to scale AI agents for Nasdaq
blurb: EquiLibre Technologies, a Prague-based startup founded by three ex-DeepMind researchers, closed a Series A led by Creandum at a valuation above $500M, the firm's largest single investment. The company builds reinforcement learning agents that trade billions of dollars daily across the S&P 500 and Nasdaq via a quant-firm partnership, and will spend most of the raise on compute to scale the operation.

title: Forward Industries jumps 17% after expanding Solana treasury to 7.55 million SOL
blurb: Forward Industries stock jumped 17% after it expanded its Solana treasury to 7.55 million SOL, more than the next three largest publicly traded Solana treasury companies hold combined. The move deepens the digital-asset-treasury trend among small-cap public companies, where firms raise capital to accumulate a single token and tie their equity value to its price rather than to operating revenue.

title: Finance and Tech Cut 28,000 Jobs Monthly as AI Takes Hold
blurb: Finance and tech firms are now cutting a combined 28,000 jobs per month, with employers citing slower hiring, natural attrition, and a growing choice not to backfill roles as AI absorbs the work. The shift lands hardest on back-office and support functions in banking and fintech, where automation of routine processing, KYC review, and customer service is turning headcount reduction into an operating-cost strategy.

title: dYdX Launches Arcus, a DEX Pairing Stock Tokens With Perpetuals on Robinhood Chain
blurb: dYdX Labs launched Arcus, a decentralized exchange built with Robinhood Crypto that pairs tokenized stock trading with perpetual futures on Robinhood Chain, the brokerage's newly public EVM layer 2. Spot trading across 95 stock tokens is live now, offering round-the-clock exposure to tokenized equities, while perpetuals covering 35 real-world-asset markets remain waitlisted.

title: Citi slashes 12-month bitcoin, ether targets as ETF flows dry up
blurb: Citi cut its 12-month base-case targets for bitcoin to $82,000 from $112,000 and ether to $2,240 from $3,175, now assuming zero net U.S. spot-ETF inflows over the next year. The bank cited stalled digital asset market structure legislation, weak demand, and treasury-selling fears, after the ETFs posted a record $4B in net outflows in June.

title: Kalshi and Polymarket’s combined volume surges 75% to $45 billion in June amid World Cup fever
blurb: Prediction-market volume on Kalshi and Polymarket jumped 75% month-over-month to a combined $45B in June, driven by heavy World Cup betting. Kalshi led the surge with an 87.4% increase to $31.5B, up from $16.81B in May, extending the platform's push to normalize event contracts as a mainstream trading venue alongside regulated exchanges.

title: Why an idempotency key isn’t an idempotency guarantee
blurb: An idempotency key only prevents duplicate charges if four assumptions hold: matching claim, preserved intent, durable memory, and a correct boundary. The author walks through a composite payment incident where a 2-second client timeout, inherited from internal service calls, triggered a retry that charged a card twice while the database recorded one payment. The lesson for payments engineers: a timeout is a third state, not a failure, and treating it as one is where double charges hide.

title: EU offers quota deal to end UK-France standoff over €5bn Scaleup fund
blurb: The EU proposed a quota-based compromise to break the UK-France deadlock over its planned €5bn Scaleup Europe fund, which aims to channel late-stage capital into European tech companies. The standoff centers on whether UK-based firms and investors can access the fund post-Brexit, with France pushing to restrict participation and the quota deal offering a capped middle ground.

title: Ethena Integrates USDe Into BlackRock's Aladdin
blurb: Ethena integrated its USDe synthetic dollar into BlackRock's Aladdin platform, giving the token exposure to the more than $20T in assets managed by institutions on the system, alongside a liquidity facility on BlackRock's tokenized products and BUIDL as the primary asset for Ethena's whitelabel offering. The tie-up deepens the on-ramp between tokenized dollars and traditional asset managers, positioning USDe as institutional settlement infrastructure rather than a purely DeFi yield instrument.

title: Ark Invest scoops up $18 million in Circle shares as stock dips 41% over past month
blurb: Ark Invest bought roughly $18M in Circle shares after the stock fell 41% over the past month, including a nearly 18% drop on Tuesday following the launch of rival stablecoin project OUSD. The purchase signals continued institutional conviction in the USDC issuer even as competition in the stablecoin market intensifies and Circle's post-IPO valuation comes under pressure.

title: Open Standard launches Open USD, backed by 140+ companies
blurb: Open Standard launched Open USD, a stablecoin for global money movement backed by more than 140 companies including Visa, Stripe, Mastercard, Adyen, BlackRock, BNY, and Standard Chartered. The pitch targets stablecoin economics directly: partners mint and redeem at no cost with no volume limits and keep reserve earnings minus a management fee, while governance runs through a partner board rather than a single issuer.

title: Congo Plans First Stock Market as AI Mineral Boom Draws Interest
blurb: The Democratic Republic of Congo is drawing up plans for its first stock exchange, aiming to channel investment into an economy buoyed by surging demand for the minerals underpinning the AI buildout. A domestic bourse would give the country a mechanism to capture more value from cobalt, copper, and coltan flows that currently route through foreign markets, though standing up functioning market infrastructure and regulation from scratch remains a multi-year effort.

title: Qonto and Pennylane: French fintech friends and foes
blurb: Qonto and Pennylane, two Paris-based fintechs backed by Sequoia and Tiger Global, have become both partners and rivals in Europe's SME market, each integrating the other's services while expanding into all-in-one banking and accounting suites. Qonto, valued at $5B and now serving 600,000 SME and freelance customers, is awaiting a French banking licence that would let it offer lending directly rather than through its current Payment Institution status.

title: UK sets landmark capital market abuse rules in crypto framework
blurb: The UK's Financial Conduct Authority finalized a broad crypto framework establishing prudential, market abuse, and stablecoin rules, with a mandatory authorization regime for exchanges, custodians, stablecoin issuers, and staking firms taking effect Oct. 25, 2027. The rules cut the K-SII capital coefficient for stablecoin issuance to 1% from 2%, allow backing pools to hold up to 5% excess assets, and require trading platforms to publish disclosure documents for listed assets.

title: Former Goldman Analyst’s AI Startup Is Said to Raise $22 Million
blurb: LinqAlpha, an AI investment-research startup founded by a former Goldman Sachs analyst, is close to raising a $22M Series A, according to people familiar with the matter. The round adds to a growing wave of AI tools targeting the sell-side and buy-side research workflows that Wall Street analysts have historically done by hand.

title: Investors Are Using Leveraged ETFs to Turbocharge AI Bets
blurb: Investors are using leveraged ETFs to amplify exposure to AI-linked stocks like memory-chip maker SK Hynix, with analysts saying these funds now magnify daily swings in the underlying names. The concentration adds a reflexive risk to the AI trade, since forced daily rebalancing can accelerate selloffs when sentiment turns and drive volatility well beyond the stocks' own moves.
